contents.name: Maguuma Jungle
headers: White Mantle Wrath: A Helping Hand

entries: The Guild Wars franchise is the flagship franchise of the Washington state based development studio ArenaNet.
The first entry into it's now 20 year old story is Guild Wars Prophecies, as it was later known.
This book aims to capture the story and the feelings of this beloved game, and bring them to the gaming table with the help of the Dungeons & Dragons 5th edition rule set.
All credit goes to the amazing people at ArenaNet and NCSoft, who have given us players such magical adventures.

entries.name: Introduction
entries: Introduction into the story.

items: {@i This chapter is designed to fill one or two sessions of play.}

entries.name: Key Plot Points
entries: The following information is key to the chapter's story:

items.name: Ascalon Invaded
items.entry: A recent invasion by the {@race Charr|GWP} has left the {@race Human (Ascalon)|GWP|Human} kingdom of Ascalon in a state of turmoil. The Charr have successfully pushed the Humans south of the Great Northern Wall, and are now threatening to breach the kingdom's defenses. The King has sent out a call of Heroes, asking for brave adventurers to step forward and help defend Ascalon from the Charr invasion.

items.name: Ascalon City
items.entry: The characters start their adventure in the largest city in the kingdom, Ascalon City, which is known for its beautiful architecture, numerous libraries, and bountiful shops. Because it is where many noble families make their home, it tends to receive the largest number of visitors and has become the cultural center of Ascalon. There they will meet many colorful characters, some of whom will become important allies, while others might have their own agendas. The city is also a hub for adventurers, mercenaries, and traders, making it a bustling and vibrant place to start their journey.

items.name: Personal Connections
items.entry: Each character has different reason for answering the call, in the first part of the adventure the characters will have the opportunity to explore their personal connections to the city and the war.

entries.name: Preparation
entries: Before running the chapter, prepare as follows:

items.name: Step 2
items.entry: Familiarize yourself with the Key NPCs table below; the characters will interact with these individuals throughout the chapter.

entries.caption: Key NPCs
rows: Leader of her own Adventuring Group
Mysterious member of Devona's Adventuring Group
Leader of the Actors Guild and betrothed to Prince Rurik
Military officer in charge of training new recruits

entries.name: The characters
entries: The characters have all arrived in Ascalon City for their own reasons, some may have been born there, some may seek glory and adventure, and others might have ulterior motives. What they all have in common is that they followed {@creature King Adelbern|GWP|King Adelbern's} call for heroes to defend Ascalon against the Charr invasion. The characters are keenly aware of the relentless attacks from the Charr, attacking the Great Northern Wall and the surrounding areas. But for now the characters are nobody special in the eyes of the King, first they must prove themselves worthy of his attention.

Each character starts in a location unique to their background, highlighting their personal connection to the city and the ongoing war. Make sure to allude to their choice of class and what social connections they might have in the city. Look at the table of {@area Key NPCs|GWP3a2a|x} in Ascalon for inspiration on who they might know and where they might be. The characters are all in the city for the same reason, but they have different perspectives on the war and the situation in Ascalon. This can lead to interesting roleplaying opportunities as they interact with each other and the NPCs in the city.

entries.name: Sir Tydus
entries: Sir Tydus was put in charge of training up the new recruits following King Adelbern's call for heroes. He is kind and welcoming to the characters, interested in bringing out the best in them. He has many connections to trainers in specific fields and will recommend the characters seek out these trainers to help them prepare for the war.

He is respected by his peers for his honesty and willingness to help. He is a skilled fighter, using elemental magic to enhance his combat abilities. He is a strong believer in the Ascalon cause and is dedicated to defending the kingdom from the Charr invasion. He is also a bit of a romantic, often waxing poetic about the glory of battle and the honor of serving Ascalon. He is a great source of information for the characters, and can help them navigate the city and find their way to important locations and NPCs.

entries.name: Devona's Adventuring Group
entries: {@b Devona} is dedicated and skilled fighter. Through her ability to lead she has gathered a small group of adventurers around her, each with their own unique skills and personalities. She is also part of the prestigious Ascalon's Chosen, a guild of elite fighters who have proven themselves in battle and are dedicated to defending Ascalon. King Adelbern is also part of the guild, as was Devona's father before her. Devona's group is made up of Aidan, the brain of the group, Cynn, the hothead, Eve, the mysterious one, and Mhenlo, the heart. They are all dedicated to defending Ascalon and are eager to help the characters in any way they can. They have a strong bond with each other and will be a valuable source of support and information for the characters as they navigate the city and prepare for the war.

{@b Aidan} is the brain of the group, a skilled strategist and tactician. He is calm and collected, always thinking several steps ahead. He prefers to spend his time out in the wilderness, using his ranger abilities to track and hunt down enemies. He is a skilled archer and is always looking for ways to improve his skills. He only speaks when he has something important to say, and is often the voice of reason in the group. He is the oldest member of the group and has a wealth of experience in combat and survival.

{@b Cynn} is the hothead of the group, a passionate and impulsive elementalist. She has a cavalier attitude toward her own death, and is always eager to jump into battle. She is extremely intelligent and has a deep understanding of elemental magic, but her impulsiveness can sometimes get her into trouble. She is flippant and sarcastic, often making jokes and quips even in the heat of battle. Together with Eve they are the youngest members of the group. She is in a committed relationship with Mhenlo, and is fiercely protective of him, to the point of getting jealous if he spends too much time with other people.

{@b Eve} is the mysterious one of the group, a skilled necromancer with a tragic past. She is elusive and hard to read, often keeping her true intentions to herself. Her best friend is a skull she found in a hidden library, which she claims is called Adam and speaks to her. She often gets in fights with Cynn, but when things get tough they work exceptionally well together. Her preferred method of combat is manipulating blood, siphoning the life force of her enemies to heal herself and supporting her allies. She is the most independent member of the group, often going off on her own to explore graveyards and other dark places.

{@b Mhenlo} is the heart of the group, a compassionate and loyal monk. He is always looking out for the wellbeing of his companions and will go to great lengths to protect them. He is a skilled healer and is always willing to use his abilities to support the group. Though timid at first, he is a deeply caring individual who values his friendships above all else. He is in a committed relationship with Cynn, who brings out the best in him and encourages to wield his immense power in battle.

entries.name: The War
entries: After over a millennium of absence, the Charr have returned to Ascalon with a vengeance, they are intent on reclaiming their ancestral homeland and will stop at nothing to achieve their goal. Unified under the banner of the Flame Legion and using the power of their new "Gods" the Charr have been able to push the Humans south of the Great Northern Wall, and are now threatening to breach the kingdom's defenses. The war has caused widespread devastation and loss of life, and has left the kingdom in a state of turmoil. The King has sent out a call of Heroes, asking for brave adventurers to step forward and help defend Ascalon from the Charr invasion. The characters have answered this call, and are now part of the kingdom's efforts to repel the Charr and protect their homeland.

In the beginning of the story, the Charr are a looming threat, with signs of small incursions south of the Great Northern Wall worrying the citizens of Ascalon. Later on in the story, the characters will venture out to the front lines, where they will see the full extent of the Charr invasion and the devastation it has caused. They will also start to see what the Charr have planned to breach the kingdom's defenses, and will have to work together to stop them.

The largest city in the kingdom, Ascalon City is known for its beautiful architecture, numerous libraries, and bountiful shops. Because it is where many noble families makes[sic] their home, it tends to receive the largest number of visitors and has become the cultural center of Ascalon.
— in-game description

entries.name: Military Ward
entries: Located in the eastern part of the city, the Military Ward is only accessible by boat or by passing through one of the two checkpoints. The first checkpoint is located inside the eastern walls of the city, and is heavily guarded by soldiers. Resources from outside the city are brought in through this checkpoint, and it is where the troops are mobilized and sent out to the front lines. The second checkpoint is located inside the city, it prevents anyone not authorized from fully crossing Balthazar's Reach, the only bridge connecting the Military Ward to the rest of the city.

Inside the ward are the barracks, the training grounds, and the armory. The barracks are where the soldiers live and sleep, they are simple and functional, with rows of beds and lockers for their belongings. The training grounds are where the soldiers practice their combat skills, they are equipped with various training dummies and targets, as well as a large open area for sparring. The armory is where the soldiers can find weapons and armor, it is well stocked with a variety of equipment, including swords, shields, bows, and crossbows. Recently the armory has begun requisitioning more and more armaments from private citizens, as the war effort has put a strain on the kingdom's resources.

Located close to the eastern checkpoint are the stables, where the warclaws used by the cavalry are kept. The warclaws are large, fierce beasts that are trained for combat, they are a crucial part of the Ascalon army and are used for scouting, raiding, and charging into battle. The stables are well maintained and the warclaws are treated with great care, as they are considered valuable assets to the kingdom.

Since becoming King, Adelbern has moved the headquarters of the Ascalon army to the Military Ward, and has been spending more and more time there. This has caused some tension with the capital city of Rin, many miles to the west. Rin is the traditional seat of power for the Ascalon monarchy, and many of the noble families have their estates there. The move to the Military Ward has caused some resentment among the nobility, who feel that the King is neglecting his duties to them and to the city of Rin. However, Adelbern is determined to lead his troops from the front lines, and believes that being close to the action will help him make better decisions and inspire his soldiers.

Training his own soldiers {@creature Prince Rurik (Pre-Searing)|GWP}, the King's son, is often seen in the training grounds, sparring with the soldiers and giving them advice. He is a skilled fighter and is well respected by the troops, who see him as a symbol of hope and strength. He, even more than his father, is determined to defend Ascalon, but not just for the sake of the kingdom, but also to protect the citizens and the people he loves. He is particularly close to Lady Althea, whom he is betrothed to. She spends most of her time at her Actors Stage on the outskirts of the Western Outer Ward, but often visits the Military Ward to see Rurik and to support the troops.

entries.name: The Rise
entries: Leading from the main gate in the south west, all the way to the checkpoint leading into the Noble Ward, the Rise is a long, wide, gently sloping street that serves as the main thoroughfare of the city. It is lined with shops, inns, and taverns, and is always bustling with activity. The most famous inn on the Rise is the Soaring Moa, a popular gathering place for adventurers and mercenaries. The inn is known for its lively atmosphere, good food, and strong drinks. It is a great place for the characters to meet other adventurers, gather information, and find work. The inn is run by a friendly and charismatic Norn innkeeper named Jory, who is always happy to chat with her patrons and share news and gossip from around the city.

The rise is built up vertically, with multiple levels of shops and residences stacked on top of each other. In some places it is hard to see the sky, as the buildings are so close together and many of them have balconies and overhangs that sometimes even connect across the street. It is said to be possible to walk from one end of the Rise to the other without ever touching the ground. The Rise is the only place in the city where the pleasure and vice businesses are allowed to operate, and it is a popular destination for those looking for entertainment and excitement. Many times an unassuming door will lead to a century old actors stage, a hidden gambling den, or a secret underground fighting ring. The Rise is also home to many of the city's most famous shops and merchants, selling everything from weapons and armor to exotic spices and rare artifacts. It is a vibrant and lively place, full of energy and excitement, but it can also be dangerous, as it attracts all sorts of unsavory characters looking to take advantage of the crowds.

One of the strangest shops in the Rise is Nicholas and Yakkington's Emporium of Wonders, a small, cluttered shop that is only open very rarely. It is run by Nicholas the Traveler, a traveling merchant who is always on the move, and Yakkington, his trusty yak companion. The shop is filled with all the things the two found on their travels, including rare and exotic items from all over the world. The shop is known for its strange and unusual wares, and it is said that if you are looking for something specific, Nicholas can find it for you. However, the shop is only open for a short time every few months, and it is always a surprise when it appears in the Rise. When it does appear, it attracts a large crowd of curious customers eager to see what new treasures Nicholas has brought with him. The shop is a great place for the characters to find unique and powerful items, but they will have to be quick to get there before it disappears again.

entries.name: Harbor Ward
entries: Separated from the rest of the city by the Ascalon City Lake, the Harbor Ward is the main port of the landlocked city. Here most of the goods from the southern reaches of the kingdom arrive, and it is also where the city's fishing industry is located. The lake is connected to a large inland water system in the south of the kingdom, connecting the city with the rest of Ascalon. The ward is a bustling and lively place, with ships coming and going at all hours of the day and night. The docks are always busy with workers loading and unloading cargo, and the streets are filled with merchants, sailors, and fishermen. The ward is also home to many taverns and inns, catering to the needs of the sailors and travelers who pass through. It is a great place for the characters to find work as mercenaries or to gather information about the war effort, as many of the sailors and merchants have connections to the front lines and can provide valuable insights into the situation in Ascalon.
Notably absent from the Harbor Ward is the presence of military vehicles, as the military prefers to keep their operations separate from the civilian port.

entries.name: Noble Ward
entries: Located at the highest point of the city, the Noble Ward not only figuratively towers over the rest of the city, but also literally. The ward houses the estates of the noble families of Ascalon, and those wealthy enough to live among them. It is also home to the city's higher academic institutions, including but not limited to the University of Ascalon, the royal library, and the Institute of Magical Research. The ward is a place of great beauty and elegance, with grand mansions, lush gardens, and ornate fountains. The streets are lined with trees and flowers, and the air is filled with the scent of blooming roses. The ward is also home to many of the city's most prestigious shops and merchants, selling everything from fine clothing and jewelry to rare books and magical artifacts.

Entrance to the Noble Ward is heavily guarded, there are two official checkpoints, one at the end of the Rise and one on the other side of the ward, but there are also many secret entrances and hidden paths that only the wealthy and well connected know about. Only those of noble birth, or those with the proper papers, are allowed entrance into it. Unless a character has a noble background, they will need to first earn their way into the ward (legally or illegally).

entries.name: Hill Ward
entries: After the Noble Ward, the Hill Ward is the next highest and next prestigious ward in the city. It is located on a large hill to the south of the city, overlooking the Ascalon City Lake. The ward is known for its beautiful views and its peaceful atmosphere, it is a popular destination for those looking to escape the hustle and bustle of the city. The ward is home to many wealthy merchants and artisans, as well as some of the city's most famous taverns and inns. It is also home to the city's graveyard, which is a popular place for locals to visit and pay their respects to their loved ones. There are also many small libraries and bookshops in the ward, making it a great place for the characters to find information.

The Hill Ward is the artistic center of the city, though most of the artists and performers are too poor to live in the ward itself, they often perform in the taverns and inns of the ward, and many of the wealthy patrons of the ward are avid supporters of the arts.

entries.name: Craftspeople's Ward
entries: The Craftspeople's Ward, situated in the north western part of the city, is the industrial heart of Ascalon City. The clamoring of hammers and the shouts of masters fill the air as the city's skilled artisans and craftsmen work tirelessly to produce the goods needed for the war effort. The ward is home to a wide variety of workshops and factories, including blacksmiths, armorers, tailors, and alchemists. People trying to find things to buy will be sorely disappointed, as most of the craftspeople have standing agreements with the military and merchants to provide them with the goods they produce, leaving little to no inventory for the general public. What can be bought here are the services of the craftspeople, who are always looking for new clients and are willing to take on custom orders.

As opposed to the other wards, the Craftspeople's Ward had an influx of people after the Charr invasion, as many craftspeople from the surrounding areas moved to the city to find work and to be closer to the war effort. While this has caused some strain on the resources of the ward, it has also led to a boom in production and innovation, as the craftspeople are constantly looking for ways to improve their products and to find new ways to support the war effort.

entries.name: Trade Wards
entries: There are two Trade Wards in the city, the upper and the lower. The upper Trade Ward houses the city's non-perishable goods, such as textiles, tools, and other manufactured items. The lower Trade Ward is where the city's food and other perishable goods are sold. Both wards are also home to most regular homes, as the trading happens usually on the ground floor, and the upper floors are used for living quarters. Most visitors to the city are likely to spend a majority of their time in the Rise, Trade Wards and the Hill Ward, as these are close together and offer the most variety of shops, services, and entertainment. The two Trade Wards are connected by many small stairs and alleys winding up and down the hill, making it easy to get from one to the other without having to go all the way around through the Rise.

entries.name: Outer Wards
entries: The western and eastern outer wards are the least developed and least prestigious areas of the city. They are outside the city walls, and are mostly made up of small farms and villages that have been absorbed into the city as it has expanded. The outer wards are home to many of the city's poorest residents, as well as some of the city's most notorious criminals. The western outer ward is known for its actors stage, which is run by Lady Althea, a noblewoman who is betrothed to Prince Rurik. The eastern outer ward is known for its gambling dens and underground fighting rings, which are popular among the city's criminal underworld. Both outer wards are also home to many of the city's refugees from the Charr invasion, who have been displaced from their homes and are struggling to find a place in the city.

entries.name: Foreign Ward
entries: The Foreign Ward is a small area wedged between the Craftspeople's Ward and The Rise. It is home to the city's foreign visitors and citizens. It is a melting pot of cultures and languages, with people from all over the world living and working there. Since the Guild Wars just ended, foreigners are still viewed with suspicion by many of the city's residents, but this is slowly beginning to change as a non-human threat brings together the people of the city. It is also home to the city's diplomatic quarter, where dignitaries from other kingdoms and nations come to conduct business and to negotiate alliances. One quirk of the Foreign Ward is that non-Ascalon citizens are not allowed to own property in the city, so many foreigners have set up shop in the Foreign Ward, renting out space from the local residents.
If the characters have a non-Ascalon background, they are likely to have spent a lot of time in the Foreign Ward, as it is the most welcoming place for outsiders.

entries.name: The Slipwalk
entries: The Slipwalk connects the Trade Wards to the Harbor Ward, bypassing the Noble Ward and running along the Ascalon City Lake. While originally built as a shortcut for merchants and traders to get to the port, it has become the de-facto way for anyone trying to travel through the city. It therefore has become extremely busy, and the flagstone path is worn smooth from the constant foot traffic. Adding to that the constant wetness from the lake, the Slipwalk earns its name as a treacherous path, with many people slipping and falling into the lake.

entries.name: Balthazar's Reach
entries: Balthazar's Reach is the only bridge connecting the Military Ward to the rest of the city. It is a very large ornate bridge, with statues of famous Ascalon heroes lining the sides. Halfway across the bridge is a large statue of Balthazar, the god of war, who is the patron deity of Ascalon. Opposite the statue is a temple dedicated to him, where many soldiers and adventurers go to pray for victory in battle and to pay their respects to the god. Regular civilians are not allowed to further cross the bridge, a heavily guarded checkpoint prevents anyone not authorized from fully crossing Balthazar's Reach. The checkpoint is manned by soldiers, and they are very strict about who they let through. Only those with the proper papers, or those who have been personally vouched for by a high-ranking member of the military, are allowed to cross the bridge and enter the Military Ward. The checkpoint is also used to control the flow of people and goods between the Military Ward and the rest of the city, as the military needs to keep a close eye on what is coming in and out of their area to prevent any potential threats or leaks of information.

entries.name: The Last Day Dawns
entries: Decide before running this section of the adventure if you want to tell the players about the Searing (See {@area Ascalon Academy|GWP3g|x}) or not. If you choose to keep it a secret, only read the second box, otherwise read the first then the second.

The last season dawns on the Kingdom of Ascalon. It arrives with no fanfare, no tolling of alarms. Those who will remember, will speak fondly of the warm morning breeze. People carry on with their daily lives, unaware that in a short while . . . Everything they have ever known will come to an end.

Standing atop a hill overlooking the Northlands, two figures watch as a Charr incursion force is repelled by the Ascalon army. The larger of the two figures, a man clad in heavy golden armor trimmed with brown furs, without turning to a smaller more plain looking man standing next to him, says, "Scribe!" The smaller man responds immediately, "Yes, my lord?" King Adelbern responds, still looking at the fighting below, "These Charr are relentless, but we shall hold the Wall at all costs." The scribe nods, "Yes, my lord." Adelbern continues, "Take this message to Sir Tydus. Go forth and recruit the strongest, the smartest. Bring to me the bravest in all Ascalon. Find me the heroes who will lead our kingdom to glory." The scribe nods again, "As you command, my king." The two figures continue to watch the battle for a moment longer, before the scribe turns and heads back down the hill, leaving the king alone with his thoughts.

Now cut to one or more characters in a situation typical for them. Let that scene play out before repeating that for each character, allowing each play to showcase what makes their character unique. After all the characters have had their moment, consider introducing them to some members of Devona's adventuring group, as they will be important allies for the characters throughout the story. Below is a detailed description of the whereabouts of each member, what they are currently doing, and what other quests they might have for the characters. If you want you can also make sure the characters have a chance to meet each other before meeting with Sir Tydus. Take your time with this part, as it is a great opportunity to establish the characters' personalities and motivations, and to set the tone for the rest of the adventure. After you are done continue with the next section, {@area Message from a Friend|GWP3a6|x}.

entries.name: Aidan
entries: Aidan can be found in the {@area Harbor Ward|GWP3a4d|x}, where he is getting ready to set sail to Fisherwoman's Retreat, a small fishing village far to the south of the city. He is preparing for a long trip, gathering supplies and making sure his boat is in good condition. Rumors of strange growths and mutated fauna in the area have been circulating, and Aidan is eager to investigate them. Characters who are rangers or druids might be particularly interested in making his acquaintance, and he will be happy to invite them to come visit him to help out. He also mentions that a buddy of his, a fellow ranger named Ben Wolfson has been scouting the Northlands and that Gate Guard Torin can get them in contact with him. Torin is located at a gate in the Great Northern Wall roughly 60 miles the west of the City.

entries.name: Cynn
entries: Cynn is particular interest to any Wizards or Sorcerers in the party, as she is a highly intelligent and skilled elementalist. She can be found in the {@area Noble Ward|GWP3a4e|x}, where she is currently deep down a research rabbit hole in the Royal Library. She is researching the history of the Charr, especially if there are any records of their deities and their powers. She has heard troublesome rumors about the Charr's new gods, especially since they seem to be giving the Charr new and powerful abilities, and she is determined to find out as much as she can about them. What made her worry about the Charr's new gods is that the construction of strange ritual sites in the Northlands has been reported, and she fears that the Charr might be using them to channel the power of their gods. If the characters ever find out more she is more than happy to compensate them for the information. She is also a good friend of Elementalist Aziure, who is currently conducting her own research at the Wizard's Tower in the Wizard's Folly, far south east of the city. Anyone trying to learn powerful elemental magic should consider seeking out Aziure, as she is one of the most skilled elementalist in the kingdom, and is always looking for promising students to take under her wing.

entries.name: Devona
entries: Devona is currently residing in the Soaring Moa in {@area The Rise|GWP3a4c|x}, where she is getting ready to set out to Ashford. Ashford has been experiencing its fair share of troubles as a group of bandits has used the absence of the Ascalon army to terrorize the countryside, and Devona is determined to put an end to their reign of terror. Any characters who are Barbarians, Fighters or Monks might be interested in helping her out after finishing their training with Sir Tydus, as she is a skilled and experienced warrior who can teach them a lot about combat and survival. She can also introduce them to a variety of powerful allies throughout the kingdom, including but not limited to her adventuring group, Farmer Pitney, Warmaster Grast, Alison the Tanner, and many more. She is also a good friend of the King's son, Prince Rurik, who is currently training with the soldiers in the Military Ward.

entries.name: Eve
entries: Of high interest to any Rogues or Warlocks in the party, is Eve the mysterious necromancer. She can be found in the graveyard in the {@area Hill Ward|GWP3a4f|x}, where she is communicating with the spirits of the dead. She has heard rumors of disturbances in the catacombs, a large subterranean network of tunnels and chambers beneath the kingdom, where many of the dead are buried. She is concerned that something is causing the spirits to become restless, and is determined to find out what it is. She knows that Necromancer Munne is conducting her own research in the catacombs, and recommends that the characters seek her out at the catacombs entrance by Ashford Abbey.

entries.name: Mhenlo
entries: Praying in the temple of Balthazar on {@area Balthazar's Reach|GWP3a4l|x}, Mhenlo is seeking guidance and strength from the god of war. He has been having troubling visions of the war, and is worried about what they might mean for the future of Ascalon. He is particularly concerned by flashes of a great purple storm engulfing the kingdom, and the image of large, powerful beings made of fire and shadow wreaking havoc across the land. He is determined to find out what these visions mean, and to do whatever he can to prevent them from coming true. Clerics and Paladins might be particularly interested in seeking him out, as he is a skilled healer and a devout follower of both Balthazar and Dwayna, the goddess of life and air. After finishing his business at the temple, he will be heading to Ashford Abbey, also concerned by the reports of disturbances in the catacombs, and hoping to find out if they are connected to his visions. He can also introduce the characters to Grazden the Protector, a powerful and skilled cleric who specializes in protecting people from dark magic and evil spirits, and who is currently stationed in Green Hills County.

entries.name: Lady Althea
entries: Lady Althea is the daughter of the rightful King of Ascalon, {@creature Duke Barradin|GWP}, and is betrothed to Prince Rurik. She can be found in the western {@area Outer Wards|GWP3a4h|x}, where she runs an actors stage. She is a skilled performer and a charismatic leader, and is well respected by the people of the city. Despite the war she is determined to bring levity and joy to the people of Ascalon, she organizes regular public performances at her stage, and is always looking for new talent to showcase. Any characters who are Bards might be interested in seeking her out, as she is also a skilled Mesmer and can teach them a lot about the art manipulating the minds of others. She is also a good friend of Vassar, a powerful Mesmer who is currently in Foible's Fair, a small outpost close to the Wizard's Tower in the Wizard's Folly, and who is always looking for promising students to take under his wing.

entries.name: Message from a Friend

entries: Throughout the day, at different times, a courier finds you, a young girl with a flower crown on her head, wearing a simple blue dress and a red cape, introducing herself as Gwen. You all get the same message from her, a message from Sir Tydus, the person in charge of recruiting the heroes for the war effort. "I have good news for you, come find me at the entrance to the Military Ward on Balthazar's Reach tomorrow morning at sunrise."

Once they arrive at the checkpoint, they will be greeted by Sir Tydus, who congratulates and thanks them for answering the call to arms. He explains that he has set up meetings with various trainers and mentors for them to help them prepare for the war. Depending on which classes the characters have chosen, they will be directed to different trainers, as explained the next chapter, {@area War Preparations|GWP3b|x}. He also gives them each an official letter of recognition from the King, which they can use to gain access to the Military Ward and restricted access to the Noble Ward, namely the Royal Library and the Institute of Magical Research. He also gives them each 50 gp to help them get started, and tells them that they can use it to buy supplies, equipment, or anything else they might need for their training. Lastly he gives the character 1 {@item Tome of Capture|GW} and 1 {@item Signet Ring of Signet of Capture|GW} for each character, the explanation of which is below.

entries.name: Tome of Capture and Signet Ring of Signet of Capture
Signet Ring of Signet of Capture
Advancement
entries: For completing this chapter each character is awarded 50 {@variantrule Experience Points|XPHB} and 1 {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB}.

entries.name: War Preparations

entries.name: Ascalon Academy
entries: Ascalon Academy

entries.name: Overview
entries: After 2 years of war between the {@race Tyrian Human|GW|Human} kingdom of Ascalon and the {@race Charr|GW} invaders from the north, a fragile stalemate has settled over the land. The Humans are exhausted from years of fighting, and the Charr have been unable to break through the Ascalon defenses. Both sides are desperately looking for an upper hand. Into this tense situation arrives {@creature Justiciar Hablion|GWP}, an ambassador from the sister kingdom of Kryta. Hablion brings word that Kryta is willing to help Ascalon turn the tide of the war, but only if the Ascalon leadership agrees to certain terms. {@creature King Adelbern|GWP} is vehemently opposed to any alliance with Kryta, but his son {@creature Prince Rurik|GWP} sees the potential benefits of such a partnership. The player characters are drawn into the political intrigue surrounding Hablion's visit, and must navigate the dangerous waters of Ascalon politics while also dealing with the ever-present threat of the Charr.

What once was rolling green field and lush forests are now scorched earth and charred remains. The land bears the scars of the Searing and two long years of war. The Great Northern Wall still stands, a testament to the resilience of the Ascalon people, but it is battered and worn from constant attacks. Forests turned to ash, villages reduced to rubble, rivers transformed into toxic tar-filled streams—this is the new face of Ascalon. The people are weary, their spirits dampened by the relentless conflict. Yet, amidst the devastation, there is a glimmer of hope. The arrival of the Krytan ambassador brings with it the possibility of a new alliance, a chance to turn the tide of war and reclaim their homeland.

entries.name: Terrain
entries: The terrain of Ascalon has been drastically altered by the Searing and the ongoing war. Once fertile fields are now barren wastelands, and forests have been reduced to charred stumps. Water sources are contaminated and unusable.

entries.name: Settlements
entries: Many settlements have been destroyed or abandoned due to the war. Those that remain are fortified and heavily guarded, with a focus on defense against Charr attacks. Most inhabitants have either fled or taken refuge within the walls of larger towns and cities.

entries.name: Military Presence
entries: The Ascalon military is stretched thin, with troops stationed along the Great Northern Wall and in key strategic locations. Patrols are frequent, and there is a constant state of readiness for Charr incursions.

entries.name: Political Climate
entries: The political climate in Ascalon is tense and uncertain. The leadership is divided over the potential alliance with Kryta, and there is a general sense of desperation among the populace. Trust is in short supply, and everyone is looking for a way to survive the ongoing conflict.

entries.name: Economy
entries: The economy of Ascalon has been severely impacted by the war. Trade routes are disrupted, resources are scarce, and many people are struggling to make ends meet. The focus is on survival rather than prosperity.

entries.name: Notable Locations
entries: Key locations in Ascalon include the Great Northern Wall, Fort Ranik, the Ruins of Surmia, and Nolani Academy. Each of these locations plays a significant role in the ongoing conflict and the political intrigue surrounding the Krytan ambassador's visit.

entries.name: Key NPCs
entries: The following NPCs are key to this part of the adventure:

items: {@creature Justiciar Hablion|GWP}: The Krytan ambassador, who is seeking an alliance with Ascalon.
{@creature King Adelbern|GWP}: The stubborn and proud king of Ascalon, who is opposed to any alliance with Kryta.
{@creature Prince Rurik|GWP}: The pragmatic and forward-thinking prince of Ascalon, who sees the potential benefits of an alliance with Kryta.
{@creature Sir Tydus|GWP}: The seasoned and battle-hardened general of the Ascalon army, who is wary of Kryta's intentions.
{@creature Captain Osric|GWP}: A loyal and skilled officer in the Ascalon army, who supports Prince Rurik.

entries.name: The Krytan Ambassador
entries: By the time the adventure starts the players have already been back in Ascalon for a while, and have made a name for themselves as capable adventurers. They are approached by {@creature Captain Osric|GWP}, who informs them of the arrival of {@creature Justiciar Hablion|GWP} and the potential alliance with Kryta. Osric believes that the players can help ensure that the alliance is formed, and asks them to meet with {@creature Sir Tydus|GWP} to discuss their role in the negotiations. Osric warns the players that Tydus is a tough and skeptical man, and that they should expect a difficult conversation. He wants to hear from them afterwards about how the meeting went.

The players can find {@creature Sir Tydus|GWP} at the central Plaza in Ascalon City. Tydus is wary of Kryta's intentions, and will question the players about their loyalty and motivations. The players must convince Tydus that they are trustworthy and capable of handling the delicate situation. He will insist on staying loyal to {@creature King Adelbern|GWP}, but is open to hearing the players' perspective. If the players can convince Tydus of their loyalty and competence, he will agree to let them assist with the negotiations. He will give them a royal message that they are tasked with delivering to {@creature Justiciar Hablion|GWP}. He does not know the contents of the message, but stresses the importance of delivering it safely and discreetly.

If they meet with Osric afterwards he tells them to be careful when venturing outside of the walls of the city and tells them that he will talk about how their talk went with {@creature Prince Rurik}. Osric then reports to {@creature Prince Rurik|GWP} about the players' meeting with {@creature Sir Tydus|GWP}. Rurik is pleased that the players were able to convince Tydus of their loyalty, and sees them as valuable allies in the upcoming negotiations. He sets out the next morning with a small escort to meet with {@creature Justiciar Hablion|GWP}, setting up the next part of the adventure.

entries.name: The King's Message
entries: {@creature Justiciar Hablion|GWP} and his escort are camped half a day's journey outside of Ascalon City, near the ruins of an old watchtower. The players are to deliver {@creature King Adelbern's|GWP} message to Hablion and ensure that it is received safely. The journey to the camp can be uneventful, but if the players are itchy for some action, roll a random encounter from the table in Appendix B: Random Encounters.

Upon arriving at the camp, the players are greeted by {@creature Justiciar Hablion|GWP} and his entourage. Hablion is a charismatic and persuasive individual, and he will attempt to win the players over to his side. He explains that Kryta is willing to provide military support to Ascalon in exchange for official recognition of the new Krytan government and trade relations between the two kingdoms after the war. Hablion believes that this alliance is crucial for the survival of both kingdoms, and he urges the players to support his cause.

His entourage consists of a small group of Elite White Mantle soldiers, who are there to protect Hablion and ensure his safety. The players can interact with these soldiers, who may provide additional information about Kryta and the White Mantle's goals.

The first soldier is Abbot Ramoth a human male {@creature White Mantle Abbot Monk Boss|GWP}, who serves as Hablion's spiritual advisor and bodyguard. Ramoth is a devout follower of the White Mantle's teachings, and he is fiercely loyal to Hablion. He is wary of the players at first, but if they prove themselves trustworthy, he may open up to them and share more about the White Mantle's beliefs and motivations.

The second soldier is Scout Langar a human non-binary {@creature White Mantle Scout Ranger Boss|GWP}, who is responsible for gathering intelligence and scouting ahead for potential threats. Langar is pragmatic and level-headed, and they are skilled in survival and reconnaissance. They may provide the players with valuable information about the surrounding area and any potential dangers.

The third soldier is Enforcer Kalen a human female {@creature White Mantle Sycophant Mesmer Boss|GWP}, who serves as Hablion's personal enforcer and protector. Kalen is ruthless and efficient, and she will not hesitate to use force if necessary. She is suspicious of the players, but if they demonstrate their loyalty to Hablion, she may come to respect them.

The final soldier is Savant Cassandra a human female {@creature White Mantle Savant Elementalist Boss|GWP}, who is a powerful magic user and advisor to Hablion. Cassandra is intelligent and analytical, and she is deeply committed to the White Mantle's cause. She may provide the players with insights into the magical aspects of Kryta and the White Mantle's plans.

Hablion invites the players to discuss the contents of {@creature King Adelbern's|GWP} message in private. The message is a rejection of Kryta's proposal, citing concerns about the White Mantle's true intentions and the potential risks of allying with them. Hablion is disappointed by the message, but he remains hopeful that the players can help change the king's mind.

He then offers them shelter for the night, and suggests that they discuss the situation further over a meal. During the meal, Hablion will try to persuade the players to support his cause, emphasizing the benefits of the alliance and the dangers of continuing the war without Krytan support. The players must decide whether to side with Hablion and the White Mantle, or to remain loyal to {@creature King Adelbern|GWP} and Ascalon.

The next morning, when the players are just about to leaver, Scout Langar reports that they have seen {@creature Prince Rurik|GWP} and his escort approaching the camp. Hablion is pleased to see Rurik, and he hopes that the prince will be more receptive to his proposal than {@creature King Adelbern|GWP} was. The players can choose to stay and witness the meeting between Hablion and Rurik, or they can leave before the prince arrives.

If they stay, the players observe as {@creature Prince Rurik|GWP} and his escort enter the camp. Rurik is open to hearing Hablion's proposal, and he engages in a lengthy discussion with the ambassador. The players can choose to participate in the conversation, offering their own insights and opinions on the matter.

No matter the outcome {@creature Prince Rurik|GWP} will offer the party to escort them back, and invites them to help him convince his father to accept Hablion's proposal. He then promises them to inform them when the meeting with {@creature King Adelbern|GWP} takes place.
Once they are back in Ascalon City the party has a few days of downtime before the next part of the adventure starts. During this time they can freely explore the city or complete some other quests.

entries.name: The Ambassador's Quandary
entries: {@creature Justiciar Hablion|GWP} is having a tough time keeping bandits out of his supplies, yet he cannot deal with the thief himself (being a Krytan in Ascalon).

Hablion approaches the players with a worried expression. "Alas, despite the current peace between Kryta and Ascalon, your King Adelbern still chooses to view all Krytans with distrust. He respects the old laws too much to do anything untoward, but I must take care not to give him any excuse to expel me from the kingdom. Therein lies my trouble.

Unable to enter the city, my companions have been forced to travel south to Regent Valley to re-supply and have been victimized repeatedly by a bandit named Torin. We would hunt down Torin ourselves, but shedding the blood of an Ascalon, even a criminal like Torin, might be a mistake. Will you attend to this bandit threat for me?"

Finding {@creature Bandit Deceiver|XMM|Torin} is no easy task. He is an accomplished bandit who has used the Searing to his advantage. He pillages the meager supplies of anyone who comes close enough to his hideout. The Ascalonian military it too short staffed to effectively deal with him.

To find him players can ask around Ascalon city or use magical means to track him. If they ask around they city they must succeed on a {@dc 15} Charisma ({@skill Investigation}) check to find a soldier who has encountered Torin before and can tell them where he went after ambushing his caravan.

Torin can be found roughly two days travel south of Ascalon City, near the military outpost of Fort Ranik. Traveling through the war-torn countryside is dangerous, use the travel rules for Ascalon in Appendix B: Random Encounters to add excitement to the journey.

Once they arrive at the location described by the soldier they must use their tracking skills to find Torin's hideout. To do so they must succeed on a {@dc 20} Wisdom ({@skill Survival}) check. If they fail they can try again after searching the area for a few hours, in which case roll of a random encounter from Appendix B: Random Encounters.

The trail leads them to an old abandoned farmstead, which Torin has fortified and turned into his base of operations. The players must navigate the area carefully, as Torin has set up traps and sentries to protect his hideout.

Inside the hideout is {@creature Bandit Deceiver|XMM|Torin} along with two {@creature Bandit Captain|XMM|Bandit Captains}. Around the compound are also eight {@creature Spy|XMM|Bandits} keeping watch.
The traps around the hideout consist of 4 {@spell Dust Trap|GWP|Dust Traps} and 4 {@spell Flame Trap|GW|Flame Traps} placed in strategic locations.
Inside the hideout are the spoils of Torin's raids: a total of 600 gp, 3 {@item Potion of Healing|XDMG}, 1 {@item Potion of Greater Healing|XDMG}, and a random magic item.

entries.name: Cassandra's Ritual
entries: Savant Cassandra is looking to purify the area by performing a special ritual, but she needs special items crafted.

entries.name: The Charr Patrol
entries: Follow Aidan in order to destroy the Charr patrol in Old Ascalon.

entries.name: Quarrel Falls

entries: Deep within the Maguuma Jungle lies a tiny outpost surrounded by waterfalls. So lovely and scenic is this place that adventurers often spend more time here than is strictly necessary to re-supply. Situated as it is just outside Silverwood, Quarrel Falls is like the eye of the hurricane; a calm place to rest before the storm begins once again.

After the defeat of the White Mantle forces at the Bloodstone, the Shining Blade is regrouping at Quarrel Falls. This scenic sanctuary serves as a temporary base of operations for the Shining Blade as they plan their next move against the White Mantle. The characters have some time to pursue downtime activities here, but the Shining Blade is nervous about the possibility of a White Mantle counterattack. At the start of each day roll a {@dice d20}. If you roll below the number of days the characters have been at Quarrel Falls, Shining Blade scouts will report that a White Mantle force is approaching and will reach Quarrel Falls in {@dice 2d6} hours.

A Shining Blade scout rushes into the camp, panting and out of breath. "Ma'am, we have spotted a White Mantle force approaching from the east! They are moving quickly and will reach Quarrel Falls in {@dice 2d6} hours!" The only Shining Blade leader present, {@creature Evennia|GWP}, quickly organize a defense, preparing for the inevitable attack. She shouts to the characters, "We need your help to defend Quarrel Falls! We can't let the White Mantle take this place!"

The characters must help defend Quarrel Falls from the White Mantle assault. The White Mantle force consists of 1 {@creature White Mantle Parasite|GWP}, 1 {@creature White Mantle Ritualist|GWP}, 2 {@creature White Mantle Savant|GWP|White Mantle Savants} and 4 {@creature White Mantle Knight|GWP|White Mantle Knights}.

After defeating the White Mantle forces, {@creature Evennia|GWP} thanks the characters for their help and explains that apparently a traitor within the Shining Blade has revealed the Shining Blade's location to the White Mantle and now the {@creature Demagogue|GWP}, a powerful figure within the White Mantle hierarchy, is commanding a massive strike force against the Shining Blade. She does not know who the traitor is, but comments that it must be someone who has been with the Shining Blade for a long time, as the White Mantle had very detailed information about their defenses and schedules. She then asks the players to head to Ventari's Refuge and ask the Centaur Elder {@creature Ventari|GWP} if he can provide shelter for the Shining Blade while they try to figure out who the traitor is.

entries.name: Travel to Ventari's Refuge
entries: Navigation through the jungle should prove not very difficult for a group of powerful adventurers. However, the jungle is teeming with dangerous wildlife and hostile factions, so the journey to Ventari's Refuge is not without its challenges. Use the travel rules for the Maguuma Jungle in Appendix B: Random Encounters to add excitement to the journey. Consult the table below for travel times.

entries.caption: Travel Times Quarrel Falls to Ventari's Refuge

entries.name: White Mantle Wrath: A Helping Hand
Ventari's Refuge

entries: Named for a wise old Centaur with a penchant for peaceful living, visitors to this outpost do their business among the roots of a gargantuan stonewood tree, the largest known specimen of its kind.

Camping just outside of the Refuge is a small contingent of Shining Blade soldiers, as you get closer a stern looking Shining Blade Councilor approaches you. "Greetings, I am {@creature Shining Blade Elementalist|GWP|Councilor Vaylor}, {@creature Evennia|GWP} has informed me of our situation. Sadly Ventari and I are not on the best of terms, could you go inside and convince him to let us stay here for a while? We need to figure out who the traitor is and we can't do that if we're constantly on the move."

{@creature Shining Blade Elementalist|GWP|Councilor Vaylor} and {@creature Ventari|GWP} have a long history of disagreements and misunderstandings, but the wise Centaur Elder does not hold a grudge against the Shining Blade as a whole. When entering the Refuge, read the following to the characters:

As you take your first steps below the stonewood tree, you are greeted by a strange sight. Sitting atop a large stone pedestal is a silver maned Centaur, who is orating to a group of many different species. You see Humans, Charr, Norn, Centaurs, and even a few Asura and Trolls staring up at the Centaur with rapt attention. "And so as I have said before, but it always bears repeating: You cannot expect the world to give you peace. You must find it... within yourself." The Centaur's voice is calm and soothing, and his words seem to resonate with everyone in the crowd. After a few moments of silence, the Centaur looks down at you and says, "Ah, visitors. Welcome to My Refuge. I am {@creature Ventari|GWP}, and this is my home. What brings you here?"

With the lecture concluded the listeners disperse, some leaving the Refuge, others tending to the chores required to keep the place functioning. {@creature Ventari|GWP} waits patiently for the characters to approach him and explain their situation. He is a wise and compassionate individual, and he is sympathetic to the plight of the Shining Blade. However, he is a staunch pacifist, and he is insistent that the Shining Blade cannot stay at the Refuge if they continue to engage in violent activities. He eventually agrees to let the Shining Blade stay at the Refuge for a short period of time, on the condition that the characters seek out {@creature Dark Oak|GWP}, a powerful Archdruid who lives deep within the jungle, and ask them to perform a ritual that will give the Shining Blade a permanent safe haven within the Jungle.

entries.name: Travel to Dark Oak's Grove
entries: Dark Oak's Grove is located deep within the Maguuma Jungle, in an area known as the Reed Bog. The journey to the grove is treacherous, as the Reed Bog is home to many dangerous creatures and hostile factions. Use the travel rules for the Maguuma Jungle in Appendix B: Random Encounters to add excitement to the journey. Consult the table below for travel times.

entries.caption: Travel Times Ventari's Refuge to Dark Oak's Grove

entries.name: Dark Oak's Grove

entries: Hidden deep within the Reed Bog, Dark Oak's Grove is a place of great power and mystery. It is surrounded by a large and treacherous bog, filled with reeds big enough to hide a giant, and the waters are home to many dangerous creatures. The grove itself is a small clearing in the middle of the Bog, with a large and ancient oak at its center. The oak is said to be the source of the grove's power, and it is protected by powerful magic that keeps it hidden from outsiders. The grove is the home of the archdruid {@creature Dark Oak|GWP}.

As you approach the grove, you hear a deep and powerful voice in your mind. "Who dares to enter my grove?" The voice is calm and commanding, and it seems to resonate with the very earth beneath your feet. As you look around, you do not see anyone, but you can feel the presence of a powerful being watching you.

Dark Oak is a title given to the current mortal soul who merged with the spirit of the ancient oak tree at the center of the grove. The current Dark Oak is a powerful and enigmatic figure, who is deeply connected to the natural world and the magic of the jungle. They are currently occupied with helping the Forgotten establish Tarir in preparation for Glint's Legacy, but they are sympathetic to the plight of the Shining Blade and may be willing to help them if the characters can convince them of the importance of their cause.

Dark Oak needs the characters to find the ingredients to create a {@item Mystic Clover|GW2}, they can provide them with a {@item Mystic Coin|GW2} to start, but to find the rest, and to be able to combine them, they need to travel to the Fissure of Woe. Dark Oak recommends going to the Temple of Balthazar further to the west, there they might be able to gain access to the fissure. Once they are in the fissure they should seek out the Forge in the Temple of War.

entries.name: Travel to the Temple of Balthazar
entries: The Temple of Balthazar lies deep within an are of jungle known as the Falls, a collection of mountains and valleys with gargantuan waterfalls connecting them. The temple itself is located in a valley at the base of one of the largest waterfalls, and it is surrounded by treacherous terrain and dangerous wildlife. Use the travel rules for the Maguuma Jungle in Appendix B: Random Encounters to add excitement to the journey. Consult the table below for travel times.

entries.caption: Travel Times Dark Oak's Grove to Temple of Balthazar

entries.name: Temple of Balthazar

entries: Hidden deep within the furthers reachers of the maguuma jungle, the Temple of Balthazar is seemingly defying the wild nature around it. A massive stone structure made of black marble, the temple is a place of pilgrimage for only those followers strong enough to survive the treacherous jungle.

As you approach the large obsidian gates of the temple, they suddenly swing open, with the sound of a thousand battles, revealing a simple room with a massive statue of Balthazar in the middle. You feel drawn to kneel in front of it and offer your supplication to the God of War and Fire.

To summon an {@creature Avatar of Balthazar|GW} so they may be granted access to the Fissure of Woe, the characters must pray to the statue of Balthazar and offer him a sacrifice. The sacrifice must be appropriate to the God of War and Fire. Depending the worth of the item and their connection to Balthazar the characters must then succeed on a {@skill Religion|XPHB} check using either Strength or Charisma. Once they succeed the avatar is summoned:

With a laud roar of flames the statue of Balthazar ignites, and a towering figure clad in black smoldering armor steps out of the flames. The Avatar of Balthazar is a massive and imposing figure, standing over 10 feet tall and radiating an aura of power and intimidation. The avatar looks down at you and bellows: "Warriors! How good of you to come! Balthazar is in need of heroes! Prove your worth to me and earn the privilege of entering the plane of eternal battle!"

He then explains that to gain access to the Fissure of Woe, the characters must shed blood in Balthazar's name. Three creatures in the temple must stage a non-lethal fight, and depending on the prowess of the fighters involved they may be granted access. The characters must choose at least two of their own to fight, the third fighter can be the avatar or another character. At the end of each round of combat the amount of hit points lost in that round are added together and multiplied by the number of {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} resources expended during that round. This total is then divided by 100 and subtracted from 30. The result is the DC for a {@dice d20} roll. On a failure another round of combat is played, repeating until the check succeeds or only one character is left standing. On a success, or if only one character is left the avatar bows, thanks them for the violence and then opens a portal for them, allowing them entrance to the Fissure of Woe. ({@i See Guild Wars, Core Adventures for details on the Fissure of Woe, the characters land directly outside the Eternal warcamp in the Great Battle Field and encounter the Eternal Weaponsmith quickly, who can point them to the Forge. Once they free the forge they get a {@item Glob of Ectoplasm|GW2} from the Eternal Forgemaster, the {@item Obsidian Shard|GW2} they get after cleansing the temple, and the six {@item Philosopher's Stone|GW2|Philosopher's Stones} are provided by the Eternal Forgemaster. Once they have forged their weapons the Eternal Forgemaster can conjure them a portal back to Dark Oak's Grove, or give them the Material component needed for the {@spell Plane Shift} spell if a character knows it.)}

entries.name: Urgent Warning
entries: After returning from the Fissure of Woe Dark Oak tells them the following:

The omnipresent voice of Dark Oak speak to you again: "While you were in the Domain of War I communicated with jungle itself. Danger approaches. A great host of White Mantle is approaching the Refuge from the west. You do not have the strength to fight them, they are accompanied by one of their unseen gods. You must return to the refuge and evacuate to the east, to the Aurora Glade. There you can open a portal to the Henge of Denravi. Secure the three shrines to Melandru by placing a Crystal Flower into the Thorn Pedestal in each one. Once you have done so a portal will take you to the Guardian's Lair. Defeat the Guardian, plant the {@item Mystic Clover|GW2} in its heart and the Henge shall open to you and your allies."
Dark Oak then casts the {@spell Transport via Plants|XPHB} on itself, connecting to the stonewood tree in Ventari's Refuge and allowing the characters to return there quickly.

Once they return to the Refuge, they see {@creature Ventari|GWP} arguing with {@creature Evennia|GWP} and {@creature Markis|GWP}. Ventari has sensed the approaching army, especially the Mursaat leading them, and demands that the Shining Blade leave immediately. Evennia and Markis are arguing that they have no other place to go. The arrival of the characters interrupts the argument. If the characters explain what happened to them and their plan, they are convinced to leave. The Shining Blade evacuates the refuge quickly, having a head start on the White Mantle by several days. Aurora Glade is a few days travel away, and every hour counts.

entries.name: Flight to the Glade
entries: The characters must navigate the Shining Blade through the treacherous Maguuma Jungle quickly, stealthily and safely before the White Mantle can catch up to them. The Shining Blade consists of around 200 soldiers, 100 civilians and craftspeople, and around 50 pack animals. They can travel 15 miles a day, while the White Mantle chase party is traveling 25 miles a day and the main army is traveling 15 miles a day. The chase party consists of the {@creature Demagogue|GWP} and enough White Mantle soldiers to make it a {@table Determine Your XP Budget; XP Budget per Character|XDMG|Hard Difficulty Encounter}. The distance from Ventari's Refuge to Aurora Glade is 225 miles. The Shining Blade group as a head start of 125 miles. At the start of each day of travel roll the table below to determine if an encounter occurs and what the encounter is, rerolling duplicates:

If the chase party catches up to the characters they tail them until they arrive at Aurora Glade, at which point they overtake them stealthily, starting the ritual in their favor. Otherwise they arrive at the same time as the characters, or after them. The advantages and disadvantages of them arriving early or late are detailed below.

entries.name: Attuning the Glade
entries: Once the characters arrive at the glade they find themselves at the mouth of a valley. Only the characters are allowed to enter, as it is warded against anyone not part of the ritual. Unbeknownst to the characters the White Mantle have found a second entrance to the glade to the north, where the wards were dispelled by the {@creature Demagogue|GWP}.

You stand at the entrance of a large glade carved into a mesa. A bed of crystals is your entrance to it, with a single green glowing flower made entirely of crystal sitting in the middle of the bed. In the middle of the glade you can spy a shrine to Melandru with a thorn pedestal placed atop it.

The goal of the characters is to pick up the flower and place it on one of the three thorn pedestals in the glade, attuning it to their faction. Once they place a flower it dissipates and a new one sprouts from where it was plucked. Opposing them is the White Mantle who are trying to do the same. If the characters arrived before them, they only start appearing once the characters have attuned one pedestal. If they arrived at the same time they start as soon as the characters start. If the White Mantle got to the glade before the characters they have already attuned one of the pedestals to them.

Once all pedestals have been attuned to one faction, the other faction gets banished from the glade, and the successful faction gets shifted to a demiplane where they must defeat the {@creature Henge Guardian|GWP} (see below). If they are successful in defeating the guardian an place a {@item Mystic Clover|GW2} in its heart the glade attunes to that faction permanently, allowing only them access. If they fail to defeat the guardian the glade unlocks again, with all attunements reset.

entries.name: Defeating the Guardian

entries: The world around you becomes less focused as all the colors start bleeding into each other and objects seem to loose their definition. You blink trying to focus your vision. When you open your eyes again you find yourself in a verdant cave, the air suddenly went from hot and dry to cool and humid. Fairy lights drift through the cave, illuminating parts of it in technicolor. Rising from a serene pond in the middle of the cave is a large serpent like behemoth. You instinctively know, this is the Henge Guardian. Just as it fully rises from the pond, water dripping from its scaled head, two thorn pedestals activate, glowing red. A beam connects each pedestal to the guardian, projecting a shield around it. As you look down you find yourself in another bed of crystals with a single flower glowing green in it. A similar field can be seen opposite of you.

The {@creature Henge Guardian|GWP} must be defeated, and a {@item Mystic Clover|GW2} placed in its heart, for the glade to attune to the characters and open the way to the Henge of Denravi. The two thorn pedestals protect the guardian from all damage as long as they are attuned to it. Once the guardian becomes {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} the pedestals attune to it again.

entries.name: Henge of Denravi

entries: As you place the Mystic Clover in the heart of the guardian, you once again feel the world around you loose its definition. As your eyes adjust you find yourself on a small hill inside a large basin in a mesa. You see before you buildings and structures entirely made of plants, who have seemingly grown into those shapes. Multiple small streams of clear water crisscross the valley, cutting the verdant jungle floor into small districts. A variety of plant bridges connects each island to each other.

Just as wondrous as the flora is the fauna in this place. You see a wild mixture of animals and humanoid species walking and talking through the henge. The humanoids are all dressed in natural clothing of leaves and other soft plant material, some of them also just being naked. While it is not bustling with activity there is a decent population here. Animals are more common than humanoids.

Coming up the hill is a Charr, wearing nothing but a small skirt made of interwoven leaves covering their modesty. They are accompanied by a deer and a moving tree. As they approach they call out to you: "Hello, you must be the Shining Blade Dark Oak has told me about, I am overjoyed that you completed the trials of the glade, come you must be weary, we have prepared an area where you can settle down for now. I am Growing Hollow, and these are my fellow councilors, Quick Taught and Happy Rain." They say pointing first to the deer and then the tree.

The trio is the council of the henge, each representing their faction, the humanoids, the animals and the plants. In addition to this role, which does not require much effort, as life in the henge is slow, and often unexciting for decades, they are also in charge of one specific area, that is their passion. Growing Hollow's passion is the shaping and building of plant structures, they have innovated how the henge looks. Quick Thought has created the Library of Lost Tomes, where she collects works from all across Tyria, giving the residence of the henge free access to her collected works. Happy Rain has a passion for anything aquatic and loves tending to the streams and ponds dotted throughout the henge. She also loves throwing festivals and parties and surprises the residence of the henge with fun made-up holidays which they simply must celebrate.

entries.name: General Features
entries: {@b Light.} The henge sits at the bottom of a basin in a large mesa, due to being walled in, sunlight only hits it when the sun is directly above it. To change this a group of fireflies have begun working as professional illuminators, making sure that the henge is constantly covered in the gentle light of their glowing. While sometimes other lightsources are used, especially in places where you would want to have some privacy, most residence have accepted the constant presence of them.

{@b Atmosphere.} The henge as a constant temperature as the cool streams and jungle floor mix with the hot air from above. Due to this mixture of moist cold air, and mostly dry hot air, clouds form inside the basin, brining natural dampness to areas where there are less streams.

{@b Language.} Long ago when humanoids, animals and plants started living together in the henge a way to communicated was needed. A group of archdruids created a powerful enchantment on the henge, that gives each sentient creature in the henge the effects of the {@spell Tongues|XPHB} and {@spell Comprehend Languages|XPHB} spells. Due to these permanent spells a strange language has developed inside the henge, a mixture of many humanoid languages, different animal species sound and the subtle ways plants communicate with each other. Anyone growing up in the henge learns this language naturally, but has no chance to communicate with anyone outside of it.

{@b Age.} Many powerful druids and their offspring inhabit the henge. Due to this, the famous longevity of those druids has permeated the henge, causing all thing to age much more slowly in the henge. For every five years that pass, creatures' bodies only age one year. This effect is less prominent on the young of a species, only coming really into effect at around young adulthood. It is also speculated that the spell that allows the communication in the henge also artificially increases the lifespan of those affected, as the druids realized they would outlive most residence. Due to this longevity a culture of careful planning has developed, with most decisions begin heavily judged by their long-term influence.

{@b Protection.} Spanning the entity of the sky, and wrapping around the basin is a powerful ward, placed there by the first druids who settled the henge. This ward prevents anyone from the outside to observe the henge, be it conventional or magical means. Additionally it makes it so that they only creatures who can find their way to the henge are those who have visited it before. Lastly the ward prevents any violence from occurring inside the henge, as long as all three councilors are alive.

entries.name: Library of Lost Tomes
entries: Quick Thought was born, and grew up in the henge and has never left it. She is fascinated with the outside world, and became a wizard, just so she could develop the skill needed to create the spell of her dreams. Her spell, the aptly named "Forget me Not", is an enchantment placed on her library that teleports any piece of text into her library, that has been forgotten by all living creature on Tyria, preserving them there. This way she quickly became the foremost scholar on the outside world, though her knowledge usually lacks behind a few decades, which does not bother the residence of the henge much.

Her library is built inside a large redwood tree, custom made for her by Growing Hollow. Each time a piece of text is added to the library the tree grows to accommodate it. Recently, after the Cataclysm and the Searing, the tree needed to expand the library into its root network as it would have broken through the top of the basin.
{@creature Livia|GWP} and eventually {@creature Vizier Khilbron|GWP} can be found here, ravenously devouring ancient texts.

Characters spending significant time in the library can research almost any topic, with older and more forgotten topics being easier. To do so they must spend at least eight hours a day in the library, making a {@action Study|XPHB} action at the end of it. Depending on the topic, the DC and what kind of check they must roll can vary. Sometimes multiple successes are needed to learn something about difficult topics.

entries.name: Fort Koga
entries: Connected by a tunnel to the henge, and still partially in the protection of it, Fort Koga is an long abandoned defensive outpost, used by Kryta to control the trade routes to the western shores of Tyria. Guilds of warred over control over it, because whoever controlled Fort Koga, controlled the flow of trade through this region. A few hundred years ago, when the jungle became more and more treacherous, the trade routes to the west closed one by one, and Fort Koga was eventually abandoned. Many years later, after the henge was established, the residence wanted a safe way to expend their excess energy, especially the more naturally aggressive species. So it was decided that a tunnel to the fort would be built, the fort be included in the protection and language spell, except the part that prevented violence, and that from that point onward regular fighting and sport events would be held there.

Now many years later it has become tradition that once a week a big tournament is held in the fort, called "The Tournament of Beasts". Originally this was a derogatory name used by residence disgusted with the violence, but the "beasts" using the fort reclaimed it for themselves. Everyone is permitted to participate in the tournament which consists of many different activities that happen throughout the day at specific times (see below). The fort has also become the defacto training grounds for the "Denravi Defense Force", a coalition of many different species convinced that the henge will eventually be discovered by forces bent on their destruction. They train relentlessly for the "Day of Wild Blood" as they call it. They initially view the Shining Blade with suspicion, but come around when they learn about their righteous struggle, admiring what they are willing to do to protect their homeland.

entries.name: Shining Blade Camp
entries: The leaders of the henge gave the Shining Blade a plot of land at the edge of the henge, close to the tunnel to Fort Koga. There the Shining Blade erected their tents, creating a small city of tarp and string. Compared to the rest of the henge this intrusion of the fabricated has put many residents of the grove one edge. Growing Hollow has noticed the unease, and has tasked their best plant builders to start replacing the tents with natural grown homes one by one. The residence of the camp were at first wary of the change, only few accepting the offer. Over time through most people saw the benefit of a more permanent accommodation and have stowed their tents away in favour of a plant home. To make the transition easier the new homes are grown in the style of the old tents, favouring large open spaces close to the ground, giving the are distinct feel even after all the tents have been replaced.

One of the first tents to be replaced, in effort to show their cooperation and thanks, was the command tent used by the Shining Blade leadership. It is now a beautiful floral building rising above the rest, with a great view of the entire basin, enchanted with the best anti-spying spells there are. {@creature Evennia|GWP} and {@creature Markis|GWP} spend most of their time there, coordinating the rest of the Shining Blade who has not made their way to the henge and planning their next steps. The character each have a building, which they can design themselves, close to the command flower, has have the other Shining Blade leadership.

entries.name: Crafter's Grove
entries: A large grove of willow trees is home to Crafter's Corner, the work area and living space for all the great craftspeople of the henge. Due to their extended lifespan the crafters of the grove are some of the best in Tyria, having spend decades perfecting their specific craft. They have dedicated themselves to the art of crafting usually heavily manufactured items out of natural materials. The exception to that rule is Ira Steelsmith, an iron legion charr, who, with the permission of the council, has started tapping into the rich and wondrous ores buried below the henge. She is one of the few permanent residence who live in a fully artificial house, though heavily enchanted to filter out any pollutants caused by her forge. While respected in the henge she does stick out like a sore thumb.

The characters can find virtually any expert craftsperson here. They can hire them to craft or help craft them specific items, though they are expected to bring their own materials if they want manufacture something artificial. They can also train in any {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} if they spend at least 30 days, 8 hours a day in an apprenticeship. While most crafters accept gold, they are much more likely to ask for favours or rare materials in exchange for their services.

entries.name: Celebration Cave
entries: A cave used by Happy Rain to hold her frequent celebrations, she can be found there after her duties preparing her next big event.

entries.name: Mabon Market
entries: A bustling market for any kind of good. Sits at the edge, close to one of the hidden entrances to the henge, where traveling merchants bring their wars from the outside to sell.
{@creature Nicholas the Traveler|GW} spends most of his time here.

entries.name: Dragon's Sanctuary
entries: A hidden sanctuary dedicated to {@creature Glint|GWP} built by members of the Brotherhood of the Dragon deep below the Library of Lost Tomes.

entries: Passage Through The Dark River
