data.name: Appendix C: Spells
entries: List of all Spells.

data.name: Glossary
entries: List of all important Things.

item.name: Scepter of Orr
item.reqAttune: by a creature which is not a Aberration, Celestial, Elemental, Fey, Fiend, or Undead
entries: The Scepter of Orr is a relic from the kingdom of Orr which controls forces from beyond the known reality and makes this outer world obey the bearer's will.

{@bold Magic Weapon.} You gain a +3 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. You have advantage on attack rolls against Aberrations, Celestials, Elementals, Fey, Fiends, or Undead with this weapon and any Aberrations, Celestials, Elementals, Fey, Fiends, or Undead take an additional {@damage 3d6} psychic damage when hit by this weapon.

{@bold Otherworldly Influence.} While you hold this weapon you are under the effect of the {@spell Protection from Evil and Good|XPHB} spell and have advantage on any Charisma (Persuasion) and Charisma (Intimidation) checks to influence Aberrations, Celestials, Elementals, Fey, Fiends, or Undead.

{@bold Magic.} The Scepter of Orr has 20 charges and regains {@dice 1d10 + 3} expended charges daily at dawn. You can take a Magic action to cast one of the spells (save DC 20) on the following table (no material components required). The table indicates how many charges you must expend to cast the spell.

rows: {@spell Plane Shift|XPHB} (Only while at a place of great connection to the target plane.)
{@spell Gate|XPHB} (Only while at a place of great connection to the target plane.)

entries: {@bold Bound to this plane.} While holding the Scepter of Orr you cannot be transported to a different plane against your will. You automatically lose your attunement to the Scepter if you travel to a different plane, and the Scepter stays behind.

item.name: Key of Madness
entries: The very fabric of reality seems to shift and bend around this oddly shaped key. It has bulbous orange head, reminiscent of a pumpkin. A terrifying face is carved into it.

While attuned to the key you can use an action to cast the {@spell Knock|XPHB} spell by touching the locked object with the head of the key and saying 'Mad King Thorn says, open!', in which case the knocking sound of the spell is replaced by the disembodied laughter of Mad King Thorn. Each time you do so you take {@dice 1d12} psychic damage and must succeed on a DC 15 Charisma saving throw or be affected by a short term madness. (See the 2014 DMG, page 258)

{@bold Cursed.} After a attuning to the item the creature gains a random short-term madness. If they stay attuned for at least 3 days they gain a random long-term madness. If they stay attuned for over a week they gain an indefinite madness until 1 day after they lose their attunement. While afflicted by any madness they do not want to separate from the item or un-attune from it. A {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} of {@spell Greater Restoration|XPHB} spell removes the attunement to the item and the desire to stay close to it, but it does not remove the madness. Only a wish spell can end them early.

item.name: Spark (Awakened)
entries: This dagger seems to be pulsing with the heat of battle, getting hotter and hotter during a fight.
'Lift up thy weapons. For you are my soldiers, and must you be steadfast, strong, and brave of heart.' is inscribed on the blade.
You can use Spark as a spellcasting focus for your spells while attuned to it.
{@bold Dormant.} While Spark is in a dormant state, you gain the following benefits:
• You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls made with this magic weapon.
• You gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your spells.
• When you hit with an attack using this weapon, the target takes an extra 1d6 fire damage.

• When you deal piercing or psychic damage you can instead deal fire damage. Additionally, any of your features that require you to deal piercing or psychic damage now also work when dealing fire damage.
{@bold Awakened.} When Spark reaches an awakened state, you gain the following benefits:
• The weapon's bonus to attack rolls increases to +2.
• The bonus to spell attack rolls and your spell save DC increases to +2.
• The extra damage dealt by the weapon increases to 2d6.

• When you give an ally a Bardic Inspiration, they can use their reaction to immediately expend that use to make one weapon attack or cast one cantrip that requires an attack roll, adding one roll of your Bardic Inspiration die to that attack roll.

{@bold Mastery: Nick.}  When you make the extra attack of the Light property, you can make it as part of the Attack action instead of as a Bonus Action. You can make this extra attack only once per turn.
item.reqAttune: by a true follower of Balthazar

item.name: Incinerator (Exalted)
entries: 'I know this isn't the path you'd have chosen for yourself — but the Flame Legion isn't just your home now; It is my gift to you. Keep this dagger close and never forget who you are: Nonius Ragesinger.'
'Lift up thy weapons. For you are my soldiers, and must you be steadfast, strong, and brave of heart.' is inscribed on the blade.
You can use Incinerator as a spellcasting focus for your spells while attuned to it.
{@bold Dormant.} While Incinerator is in a dormant state, you gain the following benefits:
• You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls made with this magic weapon.
• You gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your spells.
• When you hit with an attack using this weapon, the target takes an extra 1d6 fire damage.

• When you deal piercing or psychic damage you can instead deal fire damage. Additionally, any of your features that require you to deal piercing or psychic damage now also work when dealing fire damage.
{@bold Awakened.} When Incinerator reaches an awakened state, you gain the following benefits:
• The weapon's bonus to attack rolls increases to +2.
• The bonus to spell attack rolls and your spell save DC increases to +2.
• The extra damage dealt by the weapon increases to 2d6.

• When you give an ally a Bardic Inspiration, they can use their reaction to immediately expend that use to make one weapon attack or cast one cantrip that requires an attack roll, adding one roll of your Bardic Inspiration die to that attack roll.
{@bold Exalted.} When Incinerator reaches an exalted state, you gain the following benefits:
• The weapon's bonus to attack rolls increases to +3.
• The bonus to spell attack rolls and your spell save DC increases to +3.
• The extra damage dealt by the weapon increases to 3d6.
• When you score a critical hit you can cause flames to erupt from the creature struck. Each hostile creature within 5 feet of the creature takes half the damage of the critical hit as fire damage.

{@bold Mastery: Nick.}  When you make the extra attack of the Light property, you can make it as part of the Attack action instead of as a Bonus Action. You can make this extra attack only once per turn.
item.reqAttune: by a true follower of Balthazar

item.name: Seeker (Dormant)
entries: This light crossbow is in the shape of a dragon's wing.
'Tyria's last hope rests on the unity of the races. The pooling of their strength. It is the only way.' is inscribed on the grip.
You can use Seeker as a spellcasting focus for your spells while attuned to it.
{@bold Dormant.} While Seeker is in a dormant state, you gain the following benefits:
• You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls made with this magic weapon.
• You gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your spells.
• When you hit with an attack using this weapon, the target takes an extra 1d6 piercing damage.
• Attack rolls made with this weapon have their critical hit range increased by 1 if your target is within 5 feet of an ally of yours.

{@bold Mastery: Slow.} If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to it, you can reduce its Speed by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. If the creature is hit more than once by weapons that have this property, the Speed reduction doesn't exceed 10 feet.
item.reqAttune: by a member of the Brotherhood of the Dragon

item.name: Dragon's Wing (Awakened)
entries: This shortbow is in the shape of a dragon's wing.
'Tyria's last hope rests on the unity of the races. The pooling of their strength. It is the only way.' is inscribed on the grip.
You can use Dragon's Wing as a spellcasting focus for your spells while attuned to it.
{@bold Dormant.} While Dragon's Wing is in a dormant state, you gain the following benefits:
• You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls made with this magic weapon.
• You gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your spells.
• When you hit with an attack using this weapon, the target takes an extra 1d6 piercing damage.
• Attack rolls made with this weapon have their critical hit range increased by 1 if your target is within 5 feet of an ally of yours.
{@bold Awakened.} When Dragon's Wing reaches an awakened state, you gain the following benefits:
• The weapon's bonus to attack rolls increases to +2.
• The bonus to spell attack rolls and your spell save DC increases to +2.
• The extra piercing damage dealt by the weapon increases to 2d6.

• At the end of a short rest you can choose one of the following damage types; Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Piercing or Poison. Any arrow shot from this weapon and the extra damage of this weapon now deal damage of the damage type chosen and are considered magical. You can also change the damage type as a bonus action by expending a spell slot of level 1+.
{@bold Mastery: Vex.} If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
item.reqAttune: by a member of the Brotherhood of the Dragon

item.name: Glint's Wing (Exalted)
entries: This shortbow is in the shape of a dragon's wing.

'I can hear her. Her voice lingers at the edge of my mind like a memory I've long forgotten—the lightest touch. Bubbling beneath the ocean waves, deep below... Her voice, like a feather against my scales.'
'Tyria's last hope rests on the unity of the races. The pooling of their strength. It is the only way.' is inscribed on the grip.
You can use Glint's Wing as a spellcasting focus for your spells while attuned to it.
{@bold Dormant.} While Glint's Wing is in a dormant state, you gain the following benefits:
• You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls made with this magic weapon.
• You gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your spells.
• When you hit with an attack using this weapon, the target takes an extra 1d6 piercing damage.
• Attack rolls made with this weapon have their critical hit range increased by 1 if your target is within 5 feet of an ally of yours.
{@bold Awakened.} When Glint's Wing reaches an awakened state, you gain the following benefits:
• The weapon's bonus to attack rolls increases to +2.
• The bonus to spell attack rolls and your spell save DC increases to +2.
• The extra piercing damage dealt by the weapon increases to 2d6.

• At the end of a short rest you can choose one of the following damage types; Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Piercing or Poison. Any arrow shot from this weapon and the extra damage of this weapon now deal damage of the damage type chosen and are considered magical. You can also change the damage type as a bonus action by expending a spell slot of level 1+.
{@bold Exalted.} When Glint's Wing reaches an exalted state, you gain the following benefits:
• The weapon's bonus to attack rolls increases to +3.
• The bonus to spell attack rolls and your spell save DC increases to +3.
• The extra piercing damage dealt by the weapon increases to 3d6.
• Attack rolls made with this weapon have their critical hit range increased by 1 if you have advantage on the attack roll.
{@bold Mastery: Vex.} If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
item.reqAttune: by a member of the Brotherhood of the Dragon

item.name: The Core (Dormant)
entries: This flaming longsword is made of ornamented deldrimor steel, with a heavy core of infernal iron.
'Offer protection to the weak. Give solace and shelter to those who need it. Be ye a salve to the wounded.' is inscribed on the blade.
You can use The Core as a spellcasting focus for your spells while attuned to it.
{@bold Dormant.} While The Core is in a dormant state, you gain the following benefits:
• You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls made with this magic weapon.
• You gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your spells.
• When you hit with an attack using this weapon, the target takes an extra 1d6 fire damage.

• When you use your Second Wind feature to regain hit points, you can choose gain adrenaline equal to the result on the {@dice d10}. Once per Long Rest, when you kill a creature, you can regain one use of your Second Wind.

{@bold Mastery: Graze.}  If your attack roll with this weapon misses a creature, you can deal damage to that creature equal to the ability modifier you used to make the attack roll. This damage is the same type dealt by the weapon, and the damage can be increased only by increasing the ability modifier.
item.reqAttune: by a member of the Core Warband

item.name: Exitare (Awakened)
entries: This sleek greatsword not only seams to cleave the material plane, but also the mists at the same time.
'Offer protection to the weak. Give solace and shelter to those who need it. Be ye a salve to the wounded.' is inscribed on the blade.
You can use Exitare as a spellcasting focus for your spells while attuned to it.
{@bold Dormant.} While Exitare is in a dormant state, you gain the following benefits:
• You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls made with this magic weapon.
• You gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your spells.
• When you hit with an attack using this weapon, the target takes an extra 1d6 fire damage.

• When you use your Second Wind feature to regain hit points, you can choose gain adrenaline equal to the result on the {@dice d10}. Once per Long Rest, when you kill a creature, you can regain one use of your Second Wind.
{@bold Awakened.} When Exitare reaches an awakened state, you gain the following benefits:
• The weapon's bonus to attack rolls increases to +2.
• The bonus to spell attack rolls and your spell save DC increases to +2.
• The extra damage dealt by the weapon increases to 2d6 and can either be fire or force damage.

• When you use your Second Wind feature to regain hit points, you can choose to cause flames to erupt, damaging creatures of your choice within 10 feet of you. The fire damage equals the result on the {@dice d10} plus your strength modifier plus your fighter level. You can now regain a use of your Second Wind twice per Long Rest.

{@bold Mastery: Graze.}  If your attack roll with this weapon misses a creature, you can deal damage to that creature equal to the ability modifier you used to make the attack roll. This damage is the same type dealt by the weapon, and the damage can be increased only by increasing the ability modifier.

item.name: Exordium (Exalted)
entries: Explorers venture into the Mists, and sometimes the Mists venture back. Though far less sentient than the Mist-being Razah, Exordium takes some joy in adapting to its wielder's needs and expectations.
'Offer protection to the weak. Give solace and shelter to those who need it. Be ye a salve to the wounded.' is inscribed on the blade.
You can use Exordium as a spellcasting focus for your spells while attuned to it.
{@bold Dormant.} While Exordium is in a dormant state, you gain the following benefits:
• You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls made with this magic weapon.
• You gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your spells.
• When you hit with an attack using this weapon, the target takes an extra 1d6 fire damage.

• When you use your Second Wind feature to regain hit points, you can choose gain adrenaline equal to the result on the {@dice d10}. Once per Long Rest, when you kill a creature, you can regain one use of your Second Wind.
{@bold Awakened.} When Exordium reaches an awakened state, you gain the following benefits:
• The weapon's bonus to attack rolls increases to +2.
• The bonus to spell attack rolls and your spell save DC increases to +2.
• The extra damage dealt by the weapon increases to 2d6 and can either be fire or force damage.

• When you use your Second Wind feature to regain hit points, you can choose to cause flames to erupt, damaging creatures of your choice within 10 feet of you. The fire damage equals the result on the {@dice d10} plus your strength modifier plus your fighter level. You can now regain a use of your Second Wind twice per Long Rest.
{@bold Exalted.} When Exordium reaches an exalted state, you gain the following benefits:
• The weapon's bonus to attack rolls increases to +3.
• The bonus to spell attack rolls and your spell save DC increases to +3.
• The extra damage dealt by the weapon increases to 3d6 and can be any damage type.

• When you use your Second Wind feature to regain hit points, you can immediately make an attack against a creature within range as part of that {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. You can now regain a use of your Second Wind thrice per Long Rest.

{@bold Mastery: Special.} When attacking with Exordium is conforms to your will, transforming itself into a form best suited to the situation. When you make an attack roll with Exordium choose which of the following mastery property you want to use for that attack:

{@bold Mastery: Cleave.} If you hit a creature with a melee attack roll using this weapon, you can make a melee attack roll with the weapon against a second creature within 5 feet of the first that is also within your reach. On a hit, the second creature takes the weapon's damage, but don't add your ability modifier to that damage unless that modifier is negative. You can make this extra attack only once per turn.

{@bold Mastery: Graze.} If your attack roll with this weapon misses a creature, you can deal damage to that creature equal to the ability modifier you used to make the attack roll. This damage is the same type dealt by the weapon, and the damage can be increased only by increasing the ability modifier.
{@bold Mastery: Push.} If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.
{@bold Mastery: Sap.} If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.

{@bold Mastery: Slow.} If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to it, you can reduce its Speed by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. If the creature is hit more than once by weapons that have this property, the Speed reduction doesn't exceed 10 feet.

{@bold Mastery: Topple.} If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can force the creature to make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus the ability modifier used to make the attack roll and your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the creature has the Prone condition.
{@bold Mastery: Vex.} If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

item.name: Celestial Slicer (Dormant)
entries: This silver-bladed handaxe glimmers softly with moonlight. When looking at the reflection on the blade you can see the night sky.
'Lead through actions, not words!' is inscribed on the silver blade.
You can use the Celestial Slicer as a spellcasting focus for your spells while attuned to it.
{@bold Dormant.} While the Celestial Slicer is in a dormant state, you gain the following benefits:
• You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls made with this magic weapon.
• You gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your spells.
• When you hit with an attack using this weapon, the target takes an extra 1d6 radiant damage.
• While you are transformed into another creature, you can add this weapon's attack and damage bonuses to your attacks.

{@bold Mastery: Vex.} If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
item.reqAttune: by a circle of the moon druid

item.name: The Mechanism (Awakened)
entries: This intricate device depicts the movement of the stars.
'Lead through actions, not words!' is inscribed on the silver blade.
You can use the The Mechanism as a spellcasting focus for your spells while attuned to it.
{@bold Dormant.} While the The Mechanism is in a dormant state, you gain the following benefits:
• You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls made with this magic weapon.
• You gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your spells.
• When you hit with an attack using this weapon, the target takes an extra 1d6 radiant damage.
• While you are transformed into another creature, you can add this weapon's attack and damage bonuses to your attacks.
{@bold Awakened.} When the The Mechanism reaches an awakened state, you gain the following benefits:
• The weapon's bonus to attack rolls increases to +2.
• The bonus to spell attack rolls and your spell save DC increases to +2.
• The extra radiant damage dealt by the weapon increases to 2d6.

• While you are transformed into another creature and you are hit by an attack roll, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to expend a spell slot to gain a bonus to your {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} equal to twice the spell slot's level until the start of your next turn.
{@bold Mastery: Vex.} If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
item.reqAttune: by a circle of the moon druid

item.name: Astralaria (Exalted)
entries: This blade is magically enchanted to show the night sky on its surface, and the constellations shift and change as you move it. The blade is light as a feather, and seems to hum with cosmic energy. As you move it through the air, you can see faint trails of stardust following its path.

'Sunsmith Ouissal dreamed she gifted an exquisite axe to a woman with skin like the star-kissed sky above the Astralarium. She woke and ran to her workbench, a delighted laugh lingering in her memory.'
'Lead through actions, not words!' is inscribed on the silver blade.
You can use Astralaria as a spellcasting focus for your spells while attuned to it.
{@bold Dormant.} While Astralaria is in a dormant state, you gain the following benefits:
• You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls made with this magic weapon.
• You gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your spells.
• When you hit with an attack using this weapon, the target takes an extra 1d6 radiant damage.
• While you are transformed into another creature, you can add this weapon's attack and damage bonuses to your attacks.
{@bold Awakened.} When Astralaria reaches an awakened state, you gain the following benefits:
• The weapon's bonus to attack rolls increases to +2.
• The bonus to spell attack rolls and your spell save DC increases to +2.
• The extra radiant damage dealt by the weapon increases to 2d6.

• While you are transformed into another creature and you are hit by an attack roll, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to expend a spell slot to gain a bonus to your {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} equal to twice the spell slot's level until the start of your next turn.
{@bold Exalted.} When Astralaria reaches an exalted state, you gain the following benefits:
• The weapon's bonus to attack rolls increases to +3.
• The bonus to spell attack rolls and your spell save DC increases to +3.
• The extra radiant damage dealt by the weapon increases to 3d6.

• While you are transformed into another creature, you cannot loose {@condition Concentration|XPHB} on a spell as a result of taking damage. Additionally, you can concentrate on one additional spell at a time.
{@bold Mastery: Vex.} If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
item.reqAttune: by a circle of the moon druid

item.name: Amulet of Gentle Repose
entries: This copper inlaid bone Amulet seems to weakly radiate divine energy, protecting its wearer from the ravages of time.
While wearing the Amulet, a creature is under the effect of the {@spell Gentle Repose} spell.

item.name: Light of Deldrimor
entries: The Light of Deldrimor is a legendary torch, said to have been forged by the Great Dwarf himself, when he forged the Dwarfs in Anvil Rock, to guide their way through the dark caves of the Shiverpeak Mountains.
The torch glows in a warm teal light.
As and action, a character holding the torch can ignite an object not being worn or carried within 5-feet of the wielder, transferring some of the warding magic.
Any creature friendly to the wielder within 10-feet of the Light of Deldrimor or any flame lit by it does not take damage from environmental cold effects.
While wielding the torch the following spells can be cast from it (fires ignited this way do not have the warding properties granted by the torch):
Cantrips (at will): {@spell Fire Bolt|PHB}, {@spell Control Flames|XGE}, {@spell Create Bonfire|XGE}, {@spell Produce Flame|PHB}

Level 1 (1/day each): {@spell Burning Hands|PHB}, {@spell Chromatic Orb|PHB} (Fire), {@spell Faerie Fire|PHB}, {@spell Fire Storm|GW}, {@spell Flare|GW}, {@spell Hellish Rebuke|PHB}, {@spell Mind Burn|GW}
Level 2 (1/day each): {@spell Aganazzar's Scorcher|XGE}, {@spell Continual Flame|PHB}, {@spell Inferno|GW}, {@spell Fireball|GW}
Level 3 (1/day each): {@spell Dispel Magic|PHB}, {@spell Fireball|PHB}, {@spell Immolate|GW}
Level 4 (2/day): {@spell Conjure Minor Elementals|PHB} ({@creature Azer|MM}), {@spell Fire Shield|PHB} (Warm), {@spell Phoenix|GW}
Level 5 (1/day): {@spell Meteor Shower|GW}

item.name: Minaar's Explosive
entries: As an action, a character can throw a grenade at a point up to 60 feet away.

Each creature within a 10-foot-radius sphere centered on that point must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw, taking 18 (4d8) thunder damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
These grapefruit-sized bombs are the signature explosive used by Minaar Stonesledge, explosive expert of the Deldrimor dwarfs.

item.name: Minaar's Upgraded Explosive
entries: As an action, a character can throw a grenade at a point up to 60 feet away.

Each creature within a 10-foot-radius sphere centered on that point must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw, taking 18 (4d8) thunder damage and 18 (4d8) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
Enhanced by the infernal magics of the flame legion, Minaar's Explosive have reached a new level of destructive capability.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Salma casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 17}):

spellcasting.name: Divine Aid (3/Day)
headerEntries: Salma casts {@spell Bless|XPHB}, {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB}, {@spell Healing Word|XPHB}, or {@spell Lesser Restoration|XPHB}, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Salma makes three Radiant Burst attacks.

action.name: Radiant Burst
entries: {@atkr m,r} {@hit 9}, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft. {@h}27 ({@damage 4d10 + 5}) Radiant damage.

action.name: Holy Word {@recharge 4}
entries: {@actSave wis} {@dc 17}, each enemy in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from Salma. {@actSaveFail} 21 ({@damage 6d6}) Radiant damage, and the target has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of Salma's next turn. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

By forging connections with divine beings and mastering mystical truths, archpriests become conduits for godly intentions and other supernatural forces. Their magic allows them to work wonders, whether to share the benevolence of their faiths or to vent divine wrath. Some archpriests attract vast followings as they claim to speak for divine forces, while others undertake personal spiritual journeys and seek to transcend mortal concerns.

entries.name: Priests
entries: {@i Arbiters of the Mortal and the Divine}

Priests harness the power of faith to work miracles. These religious adherents are as diverse as the faiths they follow. Some obey gods and their servants, while others live by age-old creeds. Belief guides priests' actions and their magic, which they use to shape the world in line with their ideologies.
Roll on or choose a result from the Priest Roles table to inspire different sorts of priests.

entries.caption: Priest Roles
rows: An ascetic who keeps wicked spirits at bay.
An elder who speaks for the dead.
An exorcist who hunts wicked spirits.
A follower of a god no one has heard of.
A mediator and teacher of traditional ways.
A philosopher devoted to a concept, multiversal view, or plane of existence.
The reincarnation of an ancient faith leader.
A ritualist who uses tinctures and performances to access the divine.
A shaman whose medicines ease many ills.
A zealot who wages war for a divine cause.

entries: Shining One, light my hours. Enkindle my soul, and inspire my deeds. Chase the shadows from my path, and let me walk in your brilliance.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Evennia casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 17}, +9 to hit with spell attacks.)

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting

entries: {@atkr m,r} {@hit 9}, reach 5 ft. or range 60 ft. {@h}27 ({@damage 4d10 + 5}) Radiant damage.

Evennia is the former leader of the Shining Blade, a group which opposes the White Mantle. After the events of Guild Wars Prophecies, she steps down to serve Princess Salma and promotes Bartholos to the leadership of the Shining Blade.

During the War in Kryta, she travels to Ascalon to seek the aid of King Adelbern against the White Mantle, in exchange for Krytan aid against the Charr. After struggling for a while to obtain said help, she is reported missing, her last known location being Old Ascalon.

trait.name: Evasion
entries: If Aidan is subjected to an effect that allows him to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, Aidan instead takes no damage if he succeeds on the save and only half damage if he fails. He can't use this trait if he has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Aidan makes three attacks, using Scimitar or Longbow in any combination, he can forgo one of these attacks to cast a spell.

action.name: Scimitar
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 9}, reach 5 ft. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) Slashing damage plus 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Poison damage.

action.name: Longbow
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 9}, range 600 ft. {@h}10 ({@damage 1d10 + 5}) Piercing damage plus 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Poison damage.

bonus.name: Deadly Aim
entries: Aidan gives himself {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the next attack roll he makes during the current turn. If that attack hits, the target takes an extra 28 ({@damage 8d6}) Poison damage.
Born in: Kree Foothills
Nation: Ascalon
Profession: Ranger
Age: 32

The son of a huntsman, Aidan grew up with a quiver of arrows on his back. His mother died giving birth to him while his family was on a wagon train from Kryta to Ascalon. As soon as the boy was old enough to hold a bow in one hand, Aidan’s father packed up just the good steel heads of their tools and the two men headed off into the wild. They found a spot in the middle of a forest clearing, refitted their tools with hand-carved handles, and built their own home from scratch.

Aidan is a survivor. Nothing is out of the question if it means he and his companions will live for another day. He thinks fast and acts even faster. His inner calm—a sort of simple confidence—has a way of infecting everyone around him. Being the oldest member of the party, he has the wisdom of years his younger counterparts lack, though he never feels the need to lord it over them. He does feel a certain amount of responsibility to keep the group informed and out of trouble if at all possible. From time to time, he takes on the role of group patriarch, but only if there is a meltdown and he sees the need for his cooler head to prevail.

Maguuma Warlocks are Necromancer Centaurs that serve a supportive role in their groups.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The Maguuma Centaur Warlock casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 15}, +7 to hit with spell attacks.)

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The centaur makes two attacks, using Psychic Staff or Distracting Ray in any combination. It can forgo one of the attacks to cast a spell.

action.name: Psychic Staff
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 7}, reach 5 ft. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage plus 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Psychic damage.

action.name: Distracting Ray
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 7}, range 90 ft. {@h}14 ({@damage 3d6 + 4}) Psychic damage, and the target has the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition until the start of the centaur's next turn.

bonus.name: Charming Trail {@recharge 5}
entries: The centaur moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}. Each creature within 5 feet of the centaur as it moves is targeted once by the following effect. {@actSave wis} {@dc 15}. {@actSaveFail} 11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage, and the target has the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn.

Maguuma Enchanters are Centaur mesmers of the Maguuma Jungle. Instead of them casting enchantment spells, as their name suggests, they try to shutdown enemy party members using interrupts and energy denial skills.

action.name: Ice Staff
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 7}, reach 5 ft. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage plus 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Cold damage.

action.name: Distracting Ray
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 7}, range 90 ft. {@h}14 ({@damage 3d6 + 4}) Cold damage, and the target has the {@condition Crippled|GW} condition until the start of the centaur's next turn.

bonus.name: Freezing Trail {@recharge 5}

Each creature within 5 feet of the centaur as it moves is targeted once by the following effect. {@actSave dex} {@dc 15}. {@actSaveFail} 11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage, and the target has the {@condition Crippled|GW} condition until the end of its next turn.
Maguuma Avengers are Centaur elementalists of the Maguuma Jungle that attack their foes with water magic.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting

trait.name: Ambusher
entries: The devourer has advantage on attack rolls against any creature it has {@status surprised}.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The devourer has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the devourer's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't {@condition incapacitated}.

trait.name: Tunneler
entries: The devourer can burrow through solid rock at half its burrow speed and leaves a 5-foot-diameter tunnel in its wake.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 13}) from one of two claws.

action.name: Stinger

trait.name: Devotion
entries: Galrath has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}.

trait.name: Formation
entries: Galrath gains a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB|AC} and {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB|Saving Throws} for each ally of Galrath that is within 5 feet of him. This bonus is not added to Galrath's AC or saving throws if Galrath is {@condition incapacitated|XPHB}.

trait.name: Halt
entries: When Galrath hits a creature with an {@action Opportunity Attack}, the creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} becomes 0 for the rest of the current turn.

entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}29 ({@damage 7d6 + 5}) slashing damage.

action.name: Heavy Crossbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 9} to hit, range 100/400 ft., one target. {@h}19 ({@damage 3d10 + 4}) piercing damage.

entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 15} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}61 ({@damage 8d12 + 9}) bludgeoning damage, plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) acid, cold, fire, lightning, poison, and thunder damage.

bonus.name: Summon Ally {@recharge}
entries: Dark Oak magically summons forth a companion. One Beast or Monstrosity with a {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB} equal to or less than one other creature within 1 mile of Dark Oak appears in an unoccupied space within 150 feet of Dark Oak. The creature is an ally to Dark Oak and their allies. In combat, it shares Dark Oak's {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, but it takes its turn immediately after Dark Oak. It obeys Dark Oak's verbal commands (no action required by Dark Oak). If Dark Oak doesn't issue any, it takes the dodge action and uses its movement to avoid danger.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (5/Day)
entries: If Dark Oak fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

legendary.name: Cast a Spell (Costs 2 Action)
entries: Dark Oak uses Spellcasting.

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 3. Immediately after another creature's turn, Dark Oak can expend a use to take one of the following actions. Dark Oak regains all expended uses at the start of each of their turns.

entries: Dark Oak is a titel given to the current mortal soul who merged with the spirit of the ancient oak tree at the center of the grove. The current Dark Oak is a powerful and enigmatic figure, who is deeply connected to the natural world and the magic of the jungle. They are currently occupied with helping the Forgotten establish Tarir in preparation for Glint's Legacy.

action.name: Multiattack

action.name: Blood Strike
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 13} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}26 ({@damage 4d8 + 8}) bludgeoning damage, plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) necrotic damage. Ventari takes 5 damage.

action.name: Blood Wisp
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 10} to hit, range 150 ft., one target. {@h}33 ({@damage 6d10}) force damage, plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) necrotic damage. Ventari takes 10 damage.

bonus.name: Bloody Charge {@recharge 5}
entries: Ventari moves up to his {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} and can move through the spaces of Medium or smaller creatures. Each creature whose space Ventari enters is targeted once by the following effect. {@actSave str} {@dc 21}. {@actSaveFail} 29 ({@damage 6d6 + 8}) bludgeoning damage, plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) necrotic damage, and the target gains 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level. Ventari takes 20 damage.
Ventari is an elder of the Maguuma centaurs found in the Maguuma Jungle and the Tarnished Coast.

In his youth he was a proud warrior who fought against humans, however over time he grew to abhor the violence that engulfed the two species. He eventually set up a camp beneath the largest Stonewood tree in the arid tops of the Maguuma Jungle. However, in 1072 AE, violence once more engulfed his home in the form of the Krytan civil war. To escape the conflict, Ventari fled south, to the Tarnished Coast, where he befriended a human named Ronan, with whom he planted the Pale Tree and founded a sanctuary of peace for all races seeking it.

trait.name: Rooting Ground
entries: The ground in a 10-foot radius around the Henge Guardian is root-covered {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. Each creature that starts its turn in that area must succeed on a {@dc 21} Strength saving throw or have its speed reduced to 0 until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The Henge Guardian is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.

trait.name: Immovable
entries: The Henge Guardian cannot be moved or teleported against its will.

trait.name: Perfect Aim
entries: The Henge Guardian does not have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on ranged weapon attacks due to being within 5 feet of an enemy.

action.name: The Henge Guardian always takes its turn on Initiative count 20
entries: On its turn, the Henge Guardian can use its interaction as normal. However, it does not take an {@variantrule Action|XPHB} or {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on its turn unless forced to do so by a spell or other effect. Instead, the Henge Guardian takes one of the following Epic Actions at the end of each player character's turn:

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 13} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}22 ({@damage 4d6 + 8}) piercing damage, plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 21}), and it has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the grapple ends.

action.name: Swallow
entries: {@actSave str} {@dc 21}, one Large or smaller creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the Henge Guardian (it can have up to four creatures swallowed at a time). {@i Failure:} The target is swallowed by the Henge Guardian and gets magically teleported to one of the plant pods in its lair, and the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition ends. A swallowed creature has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions, has {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} against attacks and other effects outside its pod, and takes 17 ({@damage 5d6}) Acid damage at the start of each of the Henge Guardian's turns.

Each pod has an {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB|AC} of 15 and 50 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, regaining all {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} at the start of the Henge Guardian's turns, even if it is destroyed. If a pod gets destroyed, the creature inside spills out {@condition prone} in an adjacent space to it.

action.name: Spit
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 13} to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. {@h}17 ({@damage 2d8 + 8}) piercing damage, plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage.

action.name: Poison Breath {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 21}, each creature in a 60-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@i Failure:} 56 ({@damage 16d6}) Poison damage. {@i Success:} Half damage.

action.name: Root Pull {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave str} {@dc 21}, each creature in a 150-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. {@i Failure:} The target is pulled up to 90 feet toward the Henge Guardian, taking {@damage 1d6} Bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet pulled. {@i Success:} The target is pulled up to 45 feet toward the Henge Guardian, taking {@damage 1d4} Bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet pulled.

action.name: Epic Resistance
entries: The Henge Guardian chooses one condition, spell, or other effect currently affecting them and rolls {@dice 1d20}. On an 11+, the effect ends on it. The Henge Guardian can use this action even if it would otherwise be unable to take actions, its actions are being controlled by another creature, or if a spell or effect alters its game statistics.

entries.name: Behemoth
entries: {@i Draconic Serpents}

Behemoths are slender, serpentine creatures. In Tyria and the Mists, Behemoths are stationary, burrowing creatures that toss rocks at those who come nearby. In the Maguuma Jungle and Crystal Desert, they are often seen with burrowing insects. They are often camouflaged in with their surroundings. In Elona, Behemoths are bipedaled, vaguely serpentine creatures. Small concentrations of them can be found throughout the Vabbi region.
Most Behemoth have the following traits in common:
{@bold Immovable.} The behemoth cannot be moved or teleported against its will.
{@bold Immutable Form.} The behemoth is immune to any spell or effect that would alter its form.
{@bold Perfect Aim.} The behemoth does not have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on ranged weapon attacks due to being within 5 feet of an enemy.
The Henge Guardian is the ancient guardian tasked with protecting the Henge of Denravi.

entries.name: Charr
entries: {@i Feline Warmongers}

The Charr were once the dominat species in Ascalon, but infighting and the invasion of humans from the south drove the Charr far to the north of Ascalon. Now many years later the Charr have united under the banner of the Flame Legion, using the power of some new gods they push from the north into what is now human territory. Their advance has been stopped by the Great Northen Wall, but it is only a matter of time before they find a way to breach it.

The Charr are trained from birth to be soldiers, they form cohesive units called warbands, which they consider to be their family. A well structured warband is a terrible force on the battlefield. All Charr have the following traits in common:

{@bold Feline Agility.} When the charr moves on its turn in combat, it can double its speed until the end of the turn. Once it uses this ability, the charr can't use it again until it moves 0 feet on one of its turns.

{@bold Inspire.} The charr can inspire one other creature that can see or hear it within 60 feet. While inspired, the creature has advantage on all {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} until it succeeds on one.

{@bold Pack Tactics.} The charr has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the charr's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't {@condition incapacitated|XPHB}.

Charr Axe Fiends throw themselves into battle with reckless abandon, wielding their axes with deadly precision. They are known for their aggressive fighting style and their willingness to charge headfirst into combat, often using their Feline Agility to close the distance quickly and strike with devastating force.
Charr Ash Walker prefer to stay in the backlines, siphoning of health from their enemies and disrupting their healing.
Charr Stalkers are masters of stealth and ambush, using their environment to their advantage and striking from unexpected angles. They devaste tightly packed groups with their explosive arrows.
Charr Mind Sparks are adept at disrupting enemy spellcasting and supporting their allies by reducing the enemy's initiative.
Charr Martyrs are the backbone of every warband, using their divine powers to heal allies and disrupt enemy actions.
Charr Fire Callers are masters of flame and heat, using their elemental powers to devastating effect on the battlefield.
Charr Blade Storms use their mastery with bladed weapons to become whirlwinds of death on the battlefield, cutting down their enemies with a flurry of strikes.
Charr Ashen Claws use their necromantic magic to drain the life from their enemies and setup their allies for powerful bursts of damage.
Charr Hunters are experts in ranged combat, using their bows to disrupt enemy actions while devastating tightly packed groups with their explosive arrows.
Charr Chaots use their disruptive magic to neutralize enemy spellcasters.
Charr Shamans use their divine mandate to heal allies and devestate their enemies with powerful bursts of damage.

Summoning meteoric fire from the sky, Charr Flame Wielders are masters of area control and burst damage, using their elemental powers to devastate groups of enemies and create zones of danger on the battlefield.

entries.name: Devourer
entries: {@i Insectoid subterranean Ambushers}

Devourers are large, insect-like creatures that make their homes in underground tunnels and caves. They are widespread across the world of Tyria and are known for their voracious appetites and aggressive behavior. Devourers are typically about the size of a human, with a hard exoskeleton and multiple legs. They have large jaws filled with razor-sharp teeth, which they use to tear apart their prey. Their two claws are also formidable weapons, capable of grappling and crushing their victims. Most fearsome of all are the Devourer's two poisonous stingers, which can inject a powerful toxin. Devourers are often found in groups and can be a significant threat to travelers and adventurers who venture into their territory.

They use swarm tactics to overwhelm their prey, injecting their victims with a potent toxin and debilitating them before finishing them off with their jaws and claws. Carrion Devourers are usually the ones engaging their prey directly, while Whiptail and Plague Devourers hang back and attack from a distance with their stingers. Devourers are also known to be attracted to sources of light and sound, making them a common hazard in caves and tunnels where adventurers may be exploring. All Devourers have the following traits in common:
{@bold Ambusher.} The devourer has advantage on attack rolls against any creature it has {@condition surprised|XPHB}.

{@bold Pack Tactics.} The devourer has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the devourer's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't {@condition incapacitated|XPHB}.
{@bold Tunneler.} The devourer can burrow through solid rock at half its burrow speed and leaves a 5-foot-diameter tunnel in its wake.
Carrion Devourer are the most aggressive and common type of Devourer. They rush their prey and attack with their claws, while using their stingers to poison and weaken their victims.

Whiptail Devourer are the scouts of the Devourer hive. They are smaller and faster than Carrion Devourers, and attack from a distance with their stingers while using their whip-like tails to strike at their prey.
Plague Devourer are a more dangerous and elusive variant of the Devourer. They amplify the poison inflicted by their stingers.
Thorn Devourer are the larger variants of Whiptail Devourers. They are hulking and more powerful than their smaller counterparts, firing volleys of poison stingers.
Fevered Devourer are the most dangerous regular Devourer adventurers are likely to encounter. They spread disease and corruption wherever they go.

Rockshot Devourer are the largest and most formidable variants of the Devourer, they are extremly rare and dangerous. They are known for their devastating attacks and ability to launch rocks at their prey.

entries.name: Jade Construct
entries: {@i Servants of the Mursaat}

Jade Constructs are powerful magical constructs created by the Mursaat to serve as their guardians and soldiers. They are made of enchanted jade and are imbued with powerful magic that allows them to perform a variety of functions, from combat to construction. Jade Constructs are typically large and vaguely humanoid in shape, with a sleek and polished appearance. They are often used by the Mursaat to guard important locations or to serve as shock troops in battle. They are also known for their durability and resilience, making them formidable opponents in combat. All Jade Constructs have the following traits in common:
{@bold Immutable Form.} The jade construct can't shape-shift.
{@bold Magic Resistance.} The jade construct has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

{@bold Spectral Agony.} {@actSave int} {@dc 17}, one creature the jade construct hit with an attack. {@i Failure:} 27 ({@damage 5d10}) Psychic damage, and the target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. {@i Success:} Half damage only.
Jade Armors are the frontline soldiers of the Mursaat. They are heavily armored and excel at close combat, using their powerful strikes and durability to overwhelm their enemies.
Jade Bows are the ranged troops of the Mursaat. They fire magically enchatned arrows at a rapid pace, using their agility and precision to strike from a distance and support their allies in combat.

entries.name: Mursaat
entries: {@i Unseen "Gods"}

The Mursaat are an ancient species of powerful demons who originally came from Nayos, the Realm of Dreams. In the distant past, a faction of Mursaat were exiled to the world of Tyria, where they established themselves on the Isles of Janthir. In Tyria, 10,000 years ago, the Mursaat allied themselves with the other ancient species, the Dwarves, the Forgotten, the Jotun, and the Seers, to fight against the Elder Dragons. They eventually betrayed the other species, choosing to phase out of reality and weather the Elder Dragons waking. Much later, Soul D'Alession encountered the Mursaat and came to worship them as gods, and the Mursaat used this to their advantage to gain power and influence over the people of Kryta.

The Mursaat are powerful spellcasters and are known for their ability to phase out of reality, making them difficult to detect and attack. They are also known for their arrogance and disdain for other species, often viewing themselves as superior beings. The most terrifying ability is to inflict Spectral Agony on their enemies. All Mursaat have the following traits in common:
{@bold Magic Resistance.} The mursaat has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.
{@bold Out of Phase.} The mursaat casts {@spell Greater Invisibility|XPHB} on itself, requiring no spell components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability.

{@bold Spectral Agony.} {@actSave int} {@dc 18}, one creature the mursaat can see within 120 feet. {@i Failure:} 55 ({@damage 10d10}) Psychic damage, and the target has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition until the start of the mursaat's next turn. {@i Success:} Half damage only.
Mursaat Necromancer use their magic to drain the life force of their enemies and heal themselves.
Mursaat Monks channel their magic to heal their allies and cleanse them of ailments.
Mursaat Elementalist use their magic conjure powerful lightning effects to rip through their enemies.
Mursaat Mesmer use their magic to distort reality around them making them harder to kill, while using their foes energy against them.
Mercia was one of the three Mursaat who helped defeat the charr invasion of Kryta. They also kidnapped and indoctrinated Soul D'Alession.
Lazarus was one of the three Mursaat who helped defeat the charr invasion of Kryta. He also kidnapped and indoctrinated Soul D'Alession.
Optimus Caliph was one of the three Mursaat who helped defeat the charr invasion of Kryta. He also kidnapped and indoctrinated Soul D'Alession. He is one of the leader of the Mursaat.

entries.name: White Mantle
entries: {@i Zealot defenders of Kryta}

items.name: Treasure:
items.entry: {@table Random Magic Items - Arcana|XDMG|Arcana}, {@table Random Magic Items - Armaments|XDMG|Armaments}, Individual

entries: The White Mantle are a religious order who worship the Unseen Ones, a group of powerful and mysterious entities. Very few in the order know the true nature of the Unseen Ones, who are powerful Mursaat spellcaster. In recent years, the White Mantle have taken over rulership of Kryta after King Jadon supposedly fled the kingdom after the Charr attacked. Now they rule with an iron fist, rooting out any who are still loyal to the old regime, especially the Shining Blade.

All White Mantle troops are fanatical in their devotion and are more than willing to lay down their lives in the line of duty. They are trained to fight in tight formations, singling out troublesome targets in hopes of crushing their enemies morale. All White Mantle have the following traits in common:
{@bold Devotion.} The white mantle has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition charmed|XPHB} or {@condition frightened|XPHB}.
{@bold Halt.} When the white mantle hits a creature with an {@action Opportunity Attack}, the creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} becomes 0 for the rest of the current turn.

{@bold Formation.} The white mantle gains a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB|AC} and {@variantrule Saving Throws|XPHB|Saving Throw} for each ally of the white mantle that is within 5 feet of it. This bonus is not added to the white mantle's AC or saving throws if the white mantle is {@condition incapacitated|XPHB}.

White Mantle Knights are the regular foot soldiers of the White Mantle. They are moderately armored and armed with a longsword and shortbow, and are trained to fight in front lines. They are usally commanded by a White Mantle Zealot.

White Mantle Scouts are adept at navigating the treacherous swamps of Kryta and the confusing valleys of the Maguuma Jungle. They prefer to attack from a distance with their longbows, picking off targets and then retreating back to safety and hiding in the terrain. They are usally commanded by a White Mantle Seeker.

White Mantle Priests are in the frontline of the properganda and religious indoctrination of the White Mantle. They are trained in combat, but prefer to support their allies with healing magic and buffs. They are usally commanded by a White Mantle Abbot.

Profane rituals and blood sacrifices are common among White Mantle Ritualists. They are trained in dark magic and curses, supporting their allies by sheathing their weapons in dark magic and cursing their enemies. They are usally commanded by a White Mantle Fanatic.

White Mantle Sycophants are devoted followers of the White Mantle's ideology. They are known for their unwavering loyalty and zeal in promoting the faction's beliefs. They are usually tasked with rooting out dissent and reporting any signs of rebellion within the ranks. They are usally commanded by a White Mantle Parasite.

White Mantle Savants are scholars and researchers who dedicate their lives to understanding the ancient lore and mystical arts of the White Mantle. They are often found in libraries and sanctuaries, poring over dusty tomes and scrolls. They use devastating fire magic to destroy their enemies. They are usally commanded by a White Mantle Adherent.

White Mantle Zealots are fanatical warriors who are fully committed to the White Mantle's cause. They are often found leading charges into battle, commanding troops of White Mantle Knights. They are known for their unwavering dedication and willingness to sacrifice themselves for the greater good of the faction. They report directly to the White Mantle leadership.

White Mantle Seekers are dedicated explorers and hunters who venture into the unknown territories in search of knowledge and resources for the White Mantle. They are skilled in tracking and survival, often operating independently or in small groups. They employ White Mantle Scouts to aid in their missions. They report directly to the White Mantle leadership.

White Mantle Abbots are the spiritual leaders of the White Mantle. They are the very best at winning over huge crowds and turning public opinion. They are often found in the most sacred temples and shrines, where they lead prayers and perform rituals. They report directly to the White Mantle leadership.

White Mantle Fanatics are extremists who go to extreme lengths to promote the White Mantle's ideology. They develop the rituals used by White Mantle Ritualists, and have the ability to imbue their allies with vampiric powers. They report directly to the White Mantle leadership.

White Mantle Parasites wurm themselves into the minds of their victims, manipulating them to serve the White Mantle's cause. They infiltrate enemy ranks, sowing discord and confusion, and turning allies against each other. They report directly to the White Mantle leadership.

White Mantle Adherents use the most destructive elemental magic to obliterate their enemies. They are often found in the most dangerous battlefields, where they unleash devastating spells to turn the tide of battle. They report directly to the White Mantle leadership.

Galrath is a powerful White Mantle Zealot who commands a large portion of the White Mantle's forces. He stays mainly behind the scenes, orchestrating the White Mantle's operations and ensuring that their plans are executed flawlessly. He is a master strategist and manipulator, and is feared by both his allies and enemies alike. He reports directly to the White Mantle leadership.

The Demagogue is a White Mantle champion that leads a massive strike on Shining Blade camps after a traitor gives away their locations throughout the Maguuma Jungle. He can be fought during the Aurora Glade mission, where he is seen attempting to attune the Henge Portal for the White Mantle's own benefit.

Justiciar Hablion was one of the few people who survived the Rise of the White Mantle, and thus one of the few people who could accurately describe the events of that night. He was one of Saul D'Alessio's closest allies up until his disappearance. He would later become a very high-ranking member of the White Mantle.

Justiciar Hablion is one of the highest-ranking non-leader members of the White Mantle. He is highly efficient killer, overseeing the collection and transportation of the chosen. He takes a perverse pleasure in sacrificing the chosen to the Unseen Ones, and is known for his ruthless tactics and unwavering loyalty to the White Mantle's cause. He reports directly to the White Mantle leadership.

Markis is a spy and infiltrator who serves the White Mantle. He is currently deep undercover in the Shining Blade, leaking information about the Shining Blade's operations and plans to the White Mantle. He is a master of deception and disguise, and is skilled in both combat and espionage. He reports directly to the White Mantle leadership.

Confessor Dorian is one of the very few leaders of the White Mantle that knows what really happened to King Jadon and Saul D'Alessio. He is the public face of the White Mantle's religious authority and is respected for his wisdom and guidance. He is a skilled orator and is often found leading sermons and religious ceremonies. He reports directly to the Unseen Ones.

spell.name: Keystone Signet
entries: {@variantrule Elite|GW}, {@variantrule Signet|GW|Signet:} Short Rest
Activate the ancient Keystone Signet, recharging most of your signets and giving them extra power.
You regain all expended uses of your {@variantrule Signet|GW|Signets} that require you to finish a short rest or exit initiative, except {@spell Keystone Signet|GW}.

For the duration, when you target a hostile creature with a {@variantrule Signet|GW}, target each creature other than the target in a 10-foot-radius sphere centered on the target. Each new target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, a new target takes {@dice 4d6} psychic damage and is {@condition incapacitated} until the end of their next turn. On a successful save, a new target takes half as much damage but suffers no other effect.
After this spell triggers {@dice 1d4 + 1} times, this spell ends.
Keystone Signet

Elite Signet. All of your signets except Keystone Signet are recharged. For 20 seconds, the next 0...5...6 time[s] you use a signet that targets a foe, all other foes adjacent to your target take 15...51...60 damage and are interrupted.

spell.name: Lightning Javelin

time.condition: which you take when you see a creature within range make an attack roll

entries: {@variantrule Armor Penetration|GW}
Conjure a lightning javelin in your hand and throw it in a straight line, passing through all creatures along its path to its target.
Choose one creature that you can see within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 3d12} lightning damage.
This attack ignores cover provided by other creatures.

On a hit, target each creature, other than you and the target, on the line between you and the target. Each new target must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a new target takes {@damage 2d12} lightning damage. On a successful save, a new target takes half as much damage.
On a miss, the target and all creatures behind the target within range are also included and must make the Dexterity saving throw.
If the creature is attacking and it failed its save, the attack roll misses.

components.m: a javelin and a piece of fleece

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage each increases by {@dice 1d12} for each slot level above 2.
Lightning Javelin

Spell. Send out a Lightning Javelin that strikes for 15...43...50 lightning damage if it hits. Lightning Javelin interrupts attacking foes. This spell has 25% armor penetration and strikes all foes between you and your target.

spell.name: Rodgort's Invocation
entries: Invoke the hellish flames of Rodgort.

Choose a point that you can see within range. Each creature in a 15-foot-radius sphere centered on that point must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes {@damage 20d6} fire damage and starts {@condition burning|GW} for the duration, taking {@damage 4d6} damage. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage, but suffers no other effect.
At the end of each of its turns, a target can make a Dexterity saving throw. On a success, the condition ends.

components.m: the scale of a red dragon

entries: Rodgort's Invocation
Spell. Target foe and all nearby foes are struck for 15...99...120 fire damage and set on fire for 1...3...3 second[s].

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: The damage each increases by {@dice 2d6} for each slot level above 6.

spell.name: Symbol of Wrath
entries: Sear a holy symbol of wrath on the ground, smiting all hostile creatures.

Choose a point that you can see within range. For the duration, you create a 10-foot-radius, 120-foot-high Cylinder centered on that point. Each creature, in that Cylinder must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes {@damage 2d8} radiant damage. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage. A creature also makes this save if it enters the Cylinder or ends its turn there, or if the Cylinder moves into its space. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
On your later turns, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to move the Cylinder in a straight line up to 30 feet.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each slot level above 1.
Symbol of Wrath
Spell. For 5 seconds, foes adjacent to the location in which the spell was cast take 8...27...32 holy damage each second.

spell.name: Animate Bone Horror
entries: {@variantrule Enchantment|GW}
Exploit a nearby corpse to create a {@creature Bone Horror|GW}.

Choose one dead creature, which has not been dead for more than 5 minutes and which has not been exploited by a spell, that you can see within range. Exploit that target, and the target becomes a {@creature Bone Horror|GW}.
The {@creature Bone Horror|GW} remains for the duration or until it reaches zero hit points, at which point it will disintegrate into ash.

The {@creature Bone Horror|GW} is friendly to you and your allies for the duration. If you fall {@condition unconscious}, the {@creature Bone Horror|GW} becomes hostile towards everything instead of disintegrating into ash. The {@creature Bone Horror|GW} acts on your initiative but takes its turn directly after yours. The {@creature Bone Horror|GW} is in the DM's control.
On each of your turns until the spell ends, you can use your action to animate another {@creature Bone Horror|GW} this way.

components.m: pages of an old book

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: Use the higher level wherever the spell's level appears in the stat block. The duration increases to 8 hours (level 3-4 slot), 24 hours (level 5-6 slot), 10 days (level 7-8 slot) or 30 days (level 9+ slot).
Animate Bone Horror
Spell. Exploit nearest corpse to animate a level 1...14...17 bone horror.

spell.name: Blood Reneawl
entries: {@variantrule Enchantment|GW}, {@variantrule Sacrifice|GW|Sacrifice:} 15%
Use some of your own blood in a ritual of renewal.
For the duration, at the start of each of your turns, you regain hit points equal to {@dice 1d4} + your spellcasting ability modifier.
When this spell ends, you regain hit points equal to {@dice 2d4} + spellcasting ability modifier.

components.m: a pinte of blood, a needle and a bag

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: The healing each increases  by {@dice 1d4} for each slot level above 1.
Blood Renewal
Enchantment Spell. For 7 seconds, you gain +3...5...6 Health regeneration. When Blood Renewal ends, you gain 40...160...190 Health.

spell.name: Soul Barbs
entries: {@variantrule Hex|GW}
Curse a target to suffer each time they are targeted by another spell.

Choose one creature that you can see within range. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, for the duration, if the target is the target of a {@status Concentration|XPHB} spell, the target takes {@damage 3d8} necrotic damage. On a successful save, the target is affected for 1 round only.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each slot level above 1.
Soul Barbs
Hex Spell. For 30 seconds, target foe takes 15...27...30 damage when an enchantment or hex is cast on that target.

spell.name: Vampiric Touch
entries: Siphon the life energy of the creature you touch.

Choose one creature within range. Make a melee spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 4d8} necrotic damage, and you regain hit points equal to the amount of necrotic damage dealt.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each slot level above 2.
Vampiric Touch
Skill. Touch target foe to steal up to 29...65...74 Health.

spell.name: Lava Font
entries: Create fissures around you, which spew out lava, melting everything within range.

Conjure a 10-foot radius, 10-foot high stationary Cylinder at your location for the duration. Each creature, other than you, in that Cylinder must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes {@damage 4d6} fire damage. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage. A creature also makes this save if it enters the Cylinder or ends its turn there, or if the Cylinder moves into its space. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
On your later turns, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to move the Cylinder in a straight line to your location.
{@variantrule Overcast|GW|Overcast:} The radius of the spell increases by 5 feet.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each slot level above 2.
Lava Font

Spell. For 5 seconds, foes adjacent to the location where this spell was cast are struck for 5...41...50 fire damage each second. If you are Overcast, this spell strikes nearby foes instead of adjacent ones.

spell.name: Shatter Delusions
entries: Shatter a harmful spell on a creature to deal damage to everyone around it.

Choose one hostile creature that you can see within range. The latest harmful spell of level 1 or higher on the target ends. If a harmful spell was ended this way, target each creature in a 5-foot-radius sphere centered on the target. A target takes {@damage 4d6} psychic damage.
Shatter Delusions
Spell. Remove one Mesmer hex from target foe. If a hex was removed, that foe and all adjacent foes takes 15...63...75 damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each slot level above 1.

spell.name: Ignite Arrows
entries: {@variantrule Preparation|GW}
Tip your arrows with explosives.
For the duration, your ranged weapon attacks deal an additional {@damage 1d6} fire damage to all creatures in a 10-foot radius around the attack target.
Ignite Arrows
Preparation. For 24 seconds, your arrows explode on contact, dealing 3...15...18 fire damage to target and all adjacent foes.

components.m: glue and bat guano

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each slot level above 2.

spell.name: Point Blank Shot

time.condition: which you take immediately before hitting a target with a Ranged weapon within an eigth of the range increments of the weapon

entries: {@variantrule Weapon Attack Spell|GW}
You shoot an enemy point-blank.
You have advantage on the attack roll if the target is within 5 feet of you. On a hit, the target suffers the weapon attack's normal effects and takes {@damage 1d8} damage.
Point Blank Shot
Bow Attack. Shoot an arrow that has half the normal range, but strikes for +10...34...40 damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: The attack deals extra damage when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 1d8}), 11 ({@dice 2d8}), and 17 ({@dice 3d8}).

spell.name: Wary Stance

time.condition: which you take when you are the target of a spell which requires a melee attack roll

entries: {@variantrule Stance|GW}
You enter a stance, to block any incoming melee spell.

For the duration, if you are the target of a spell which requires a melee attack roll, the attack roll misses you, your initiative gets increased by {@dice 1d4} and you gain {@dice 1d4} strikes of adrenalin.
Wary Stance
Stance. For 1...5...6 second[s], you block any attack skills used against you. For each successful block, you gain adrenaline and 5 Energy. Wary Stance ends if you use a skill.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The adrenaline gain increases by {@dice 1d4} for each slot level above 1 and the increase in initiative increases by {@dice 1d4} for each slot level above 1.

spell.name: Predator's Pounce
entries: {@variantrule Companion|GW}
Command your companion to attempt a Predator's Pounce.

Choose one companion that can hear you within range. The companion uses its reaction to make an attack against a creature of your choice that the companion can see within the companion's range. On a hit, the target suffers the attack's normal effects and takes {@damage 2d10} damage, and the beast regains hit points equal to {@dice 2d6} + your spellcasting ability modifier.
Predator's Pounce
Pet Attack. Your animal companion attempts a Predator's Pounce that deals +5...29...35 damage. If that attack hits, your animal companion gains 5...41...50 Health.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: The number of attacks increases by 1 for each slot level above 1.

spell.name: Restore Life
entries: You touch a creature that has died within the last minute. The target returns to life with 40% of their maximum hit points. This spell can't return to life a creature that has died of old age, nor can it restore any missing body parts.
Restore Life
Spell. Touch the body of a fallen party member. Target party member is returned to life with 20...56...65% Health and 42...80...90% Energy.

components.m: gems worth 300 gp, which the spell consumes

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: The % increases by 10%  for each slot level above 3.

spell.name: Insidious Parasite
entries: {@variantrule Hex|GW}
You magically implant a life-sucking parasite.

Choose one creature that you can see within range. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save for the duration when the target succeeds on an attack roll, the target takes {@damage 2d8} necrotic damage, and you regain hit points equal to the amount of necrotic damage dealt. On a successful save, the target is affected for 1 round only.
Insidious Parasite
Hex Spell. For 5...13...15 seconds, whenever target foe hits with an attack, you steal up to 15...39...45 Health from that foe.

components.m: a phial of swamp water

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each slot level above 2.

spell.name: Physical Resistance
entries: {@variantrule Stance|GW}
Enhance your physical defense at the cost of becoming vulnerable to elemental damage.
For the duration, you have resistance to bludgeoning, piercing and slashing damage, but have vulnerability to acid, cold, fire, force, lightning, and thunder damage.
Physical Resistance
Stance. For 30...78...90 seconds, You gain +40 armor against physical damage, but you lose 24...14...12 armor against elemental damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: The duration increases by 1 round for each slot level above 2.

spell.name: Heal Area
entries: Heal every creature in an area around you.
Target each creature within a 10-foot Emanation. A target regains hit points equal to {@dice 4d4} + your spellcasting ability modifier.
Heal Area
Spell. Heal yourself and all adjacent creatures for 30...150...180 points.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: The healing increases by {@dice 2d4} for each slot level above 2.

spell.name: Chaos Storm
entries: Conjure a storm of chaotic energy, harming and draining the energy from an area for multiple rounds.

Choose a point that you can see within range. For the duration, you create a 10-foot Cube centered on that point. Each creature in that Cube must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes {@damage 2d8} damage. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage. Choose one of the d8s. The number rolled on that die determines the spell's damage type, as shown below.
If you roll the same number on two or more d8s, a target's initiative gets reduced by the number.
A creature also makes this save if it enters the Cube or ends its turn there, or if the Cube moves into its space. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
On your later turns, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to move the Cube in a straight line up to 30 feet.
Chaos Storm
Spell. Create a Chaos Storm at target foe's location that lasts for 10 seconds. Each second, foes adjacent to this location take 5...21...25 damage and lose 0...2...2 Energy.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each slot level above 2.

spell.name: Distortion
entries: {@variantrule Stance|GW}
You magically distort space around you, making attacks miss you.
For the duration, you have a +3 bonus to AC. If an attack misses you because of this bonus, your initiative gets reduced by {@dice 2d4} or this spell ends.
Initiative cannot be reduced below 0 with this spell.
Distortion
Stance. For 1...4...5 second[s], you have a 75% chance to block attacks. Whenever you block an attack this way, you lose 2 Energy or Distortion ends.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The AC bonus increases by 1 for every two slot level above 2.

spell.name: "Fear Me!"
entries: {@variantrule Adrenaline|GW|Adrenaline:} 4, {@variantrule Shout|GW}
Intimidate nearby creatures to give you an opportunity to slip past their defenses, reducing their initiative and increasing your chance to critically hit them.

Target each creature other than you that can hear you within range. Each target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a target's initiative gets reduced by {@dice 1d8}, and the number you need to roll to score a critical hit against a target gets reduced by {@dice 1d4} for the duration. On a successful save, the target is affected for 1 round only.
At the end of each of its turns, a target can make a Wisdom saving throw. On a success, the spell ends.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: The reduction of initiative increases by {@dice 1d8} for each slot level above 3.
"Fear Me!"
Shout. All nearby foes lose 1...3...4 Energy. For 1...12...15 second[s], your melee attacks gain +5...25...30% chance of a critical hit against stationary foes.

spell.name: Eruption
